questions.stem: In preparing consolidated financial statements, which of the following is eliminated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: External revenue of the subsidiary

choices.label: B
choices.text: Intercompany sales and the related cost of goods sold

choices.label: C
choices.text: Depreciation expense of the subsidiary

choices.label: D
choices.text: Income tax expense of the subsidiary

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-1, all intercompany transactions must be eliminated in consolidation. This includes intercompany sales/purchases, intercompany receivables/payables, intercompany interest, and unrealized profits in intercompany inventory. External transactions of both parent and subsidiary are reported in full.
questions.explanationJa: 連結では全ての会社間取引を消去する。対象は会社間売上・仕入、会社間債権・債務、会社間利息、会社間在庫の未実現利益である。親会社・子会社の外部取引は全額計上する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 810, where is the noncontrolling interest (NCI) presented in the consolidated balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a liability between long-term debt and equity

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a separate component within stockholders' equity

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a contra-asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: In the mezzanine section between liabilities and equity

questions.explanation: ASC 810-10-45-16 requires that the noncontrolling interest be reported in the consolidated balance sheet within equity, separate from the parent's equity. On the income statement, net income is attributed to both the parent and the NCI. This treatment reflects that the NCI represents an ownership interest, not a liability.
questions.explanationJa: NCI（非支配持分）は連結貸借対照表の株主資本内に、親会社持分と区分して表示する。損益計算書では当期純利益を親会社とNCIの両方に帰属させる。NCIは負債ではなく所有持分であることを反映した処理である。

questions.stem: Under ASC 805, acquired identifiable assets and assumed liabilities in a business combination are measured at:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Book value on the acquiree's books

choices.label: B
choices.text: Fair value at the acquisition date

choices.label: C
choices.text: Historical cost to the acquiree

choices.label: D
choices.text: The lower of book value or fair value

questions.explanation: ASC 805-20-30-1 requires that identifiable assets acquired, liabilities assumed, and any noncontrolling interest in the acquiree be recognized at their acquisition-date fair values. This includes assets not previously recorded by the acquiree (such as internally developed intangibles) if they meet the recognition criteria.
questions.explanationJa: 取得法では、取得した識別可能資産・引受負債・NCIを取得日の公正価値で認識する。認識要件を満たせば、被取得企業が以前計上していなかった資産（自己創設無形資産等）も含まれる。

questions.stem: Parent Co acquires 100% of Sub Co for $2,000,000. The fair value of Sub's identifiable net assets is $1,700,000. What amount of goodwill is recognized?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-30-30-1, goodwill is measured as the excess of the consideration transferred over the net of the acquisition-date fair values of the identifiable assets acquired and liabilities assumed. Goodwill = $2,000,000 - $1,700,000 = $300,000.
questions.explanationJa: のれん = 移転対価 - 識別可能純資産の取得日公正価値。のれん = $2,000,000 - $1,700,000 = $300,000。

questions.stem: Parent acquires 100% of Target for $800,000. Fair value of Target's identifiable net assets is $900,000. How is the $100,000 difference reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As negative goodwill on the balance sheet

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a gain in the income statement after reassessing whether all assets/liabilities are identified

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a reduction of long-lived assets acquired

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred and amortized over the expected benefit period

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-30-25-2, if the fair value of net assets exceeds the consideration transferred (a bargain purchase), the acquirer must first reassess whether all assets and liabilities have been properly identified and measured. After reassessment, any remaining excess is recognized as a gain in earnings on the acquisition date.
questions.explanationJa: 純資産の公正価値が移転対価を超える場合（割安購入/バーゲンパーチェス）、まず全ての資産・負債が適切に識別・測定されたか再評価する。再評価後もなお残る超過額は、取得日に利益として損益認識する。

questions.stem: Parent sold inventory to Sub for $100,000 (cost of $70,000). At year-end, Sub still holds 40% of this inventory. What unrealized profit must be eliminated in consolidation?
questions.explanation: Total intercompany profit = $100,000 - $70,000 = $30,000. Unrealized profit (still in ending inventory) = $30,000 × 40% = $12,000. This $12,000 must be eliminated because from the consolidated entity's perspective, the inventory cost is $70,000 × 40% = $28,000, not $40,000 (40% × $100,000).

questions.stem: Under ASC 810-10, which entity is required to consolidate a variable interest entity (VIE)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity with the largest equity investment

choices.label: B
choices.text: The primary beneficiary—the entity with power to direct activities that most significantly impact the VIE's economic performance and the obligation to absorb losses or right to receive benefits

choices.label: C
choices.text: Any entity with a variable interest

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entity that created the VIE

questions.explanation: ASC 810-10-25-38A defines the primary beneficiary as the entity that has: (1) the power to direct the activities of the VIE that most significantly impact its economic performance, and (2) the obligation to absorb losses or the right to receive benefits that could potentially be significant to the VIE. Both conditions must be met.
questions.explanationJa: 主たる受益者（primary beneficiary）は次の2条件を両方満たす企業である：(1) VIEの経済的業績に最も重要な影響を与える活動を指揮する力、(2) VIEにとって重要となり得る損失吸収義務または便益受領権。いずれか一方だけでは不十分である。

questions.stem: Under ASC 805-50, when is push-down accounting permitted?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only when the subsidiary is wholly owned

choices.label: B
choices.text: When a change-in-control event occurs, at the subsidiary's election

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if required by the SEC

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the parent owns more than 80% of the subsidiary

questions.explanation: ASC 805-50-25-4 permits (but does not require) an acquired entity to elect push-down accounting in its separate financial statements when a change-in-control event occurs. Push-down accounting adjusts the subsidiary's assets and liabilities to the new basis (fair values) established in the acquisition.
questions.explanationJa: 支配の変更が発生した場合、被取得企業はその個別財務諸表でプッシュダウン会計を選択適用できる（任意）。プッシュダウン会計では、子会社の資産・負債を取得時の公正価値（新基準額）に調整する。

questions.stem: Parent owns 80% of Sub and sells a 30% interest, reducing its ownership to 50% and losing control. How should Parent account for this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a gain or loss on the 30% sold only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deconsolidate Sub, recognize a gain/loss on the entire interest (measuring the retained 50% at fair value)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue to consolidate Sub

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record the transaction as an equity transaction with no gain or loss

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-40-3A, when a parent loses control of a subsidiary, it deconsolidates the subsidiary's assets and liabilities, recognizes any retained noncontrolling investment at fair value, and recognizes a gain or loss on the transaction that includes the remeasurement of any retained interest to fair value.
questions.explanationJa: 親会社が子会社の支配を喪失した場合、子会社の資産・負債を連結除外し、保持する投資を公正価値で認識する。保持持分の公正価値再測定を含む取引全体の損益を認識する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 805, how should acquisition-related costs (legal fees, due diligence costs, finder's fees) be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalized as part of goodwill

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expensed as incurred

choices.label: C
choices.text: Added to the consideration transferred

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred and amortized over the expected benefit period

questions.explanation: ASC 805-10-25-23 requires that acquisition-related costs (advisory, legal, accounting, valuation, and other professional fees) be expensed in the periods in which the costs are incurred. These costs are not part of the consideration transferred and are not capitalized as goodwill. The only exception is costs to issue debt or equity securities, which follow their respective guidance.
questions.explanationJa: 取得関連費用（アドバイザリー・法務・会計・評価等の専門家報酬）は発生時に費用処理する。移転対価の一部ではなく、のれんにも算入しない。例外は負債証券・株式証券の発行費用のみであり、それぞれの該当ガイダンスに従う。

questions.stem: Parker Corp acquires 80% of Stone Inc for $4,800,000 cash. On the acquisition date, Stone's identifiable net assets have a fair value of $5,200,000. The fair value of the 20% noncontrolling interest is $1,200,000. What amount of goodwill should be recognized in the consolidated financial statements?
questions.explanation: Under the full goodwill method (ASC 805-30-30-1), goodwill = (Consideration transferred + Fair value of NCI) − Fair value of identifiable net assets. Goodwill = ($4,800,000 + $1,200,000) − $5,200,000 = $6,000,000 − $5,200,000 = $800,000.

questions.stem: Parent owns 75% of Sub acquired at the beginning of Year 1. Sub reports net income of $600,000 for Year 1. Sub's identifiable net assets included equipment with a fair value $200,000 above book value at acquisition (remaining useful life 10 years, straight-line). What is the noncontrolling interest's share of Sub's adjusted income for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Sub's adjusted income = Reported income − Excess depreciation = $600,000 − ($200,000 ÷ 10) = $600,000 − $20,000 = $580,000. NCI share = $580,000 × 25% = $145,000.

questions.stem: Parent (90% owner) sold inventory to Sub during Year 1 at a price of $500,000 (cost $350,000). At December 31, Year 1, Sub still holds 60% of this inventory unsold. What amount of unrealized intercompany profit must be eliminated from consolidated ending inventory?
questions.explanation: Total intercompany profit = $500,000 − $350,000 = $150,000. Unsold portion = 60%. Unrealized profit to eliminate = $150,000 × 60% = $90,000. For downstream sales (parent to sub), 100% of unrealized profit is eliminated regardless of ownership percentage.
questions.explanationJa: 会社間利益合計 = $500,000 - $350,000 = $150,000。未販売割合 = 60%。消去すべき未実現利益 = $150,000 × 60% = $90,000。ダウンストリーム（親→子）では所有割合に関係なく未実現利益の100%を消去する。

questions.stem: Sub (80% owned by Parent) sold land to Parent for $750,000. Sub's original cost was $500,000. On the consolidated worksheet, how much of the unrealized gain is allocated to the noncontrolling interest?
questions.explanation: Unrealized gain on upstream sale = $750,000 − $500,000 = $250,000. For upstream sales (sub to parent), the elimination is allocated between the parent and NCI based on ownership percentages. NCI allocation = $250,000 × 20% = $50,000.
questions.explanationJa: アップストリーム売却の未実現利益 = $750,000 - $500,000 = $250,000。アップストリーム（子→親）では消去額を所有割合に基づき親会社とNCIに配分する。NCI配分 = $250,000 × 20% = $50,000。

questions.stem: Parent acquired 100% of Sub on April 1, Year 1. Sub's revenue and expenses are earned evenly throughout the year. Sub reported total revenue of $2,400,000 and total expenses of $1,800,000 for the full calendar year. What amounts are included in the Year 1 consolidated income statement for Sub's operations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Revenue $2,400,000; Expenses $1,800,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Revenue $1,800,000; Expenses $1,350,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Revenue $600,000; Expenses $450,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Revenue $1,200,000; Expenses $900,000

questions.explanation: Only post-acquisition results are included in consolidation. From April 1 to December 31 = 9 months out of 12. Revenue = $2,400,000 × (9/12) = $1,800,000. Expenses = $1,800,000 × (9/12) = $1,350,000.

questions.stem: Parent held a 30% equity-method investment in Target (carrying amount $900,000). Parent then acquires the remaining 70% for $3,500,000, gaining control. On the acquisition date, the fair value of the previously held 30% interest is $1,500,000. What gain does Parent recognize on the remeasurement of the previously held interest?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-10, when control is obtained in a step acquisition, the acquirer remeasures any previously held equity interest at acquisition-date fair value. Gain = Fair value − Carrying amount = $1,500,000 − $900,000 = $600,000. This gain is recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 段階取得で支配を獲得した場合、従前保有持分を取得日の公正価値で再測定する。利益 = 公正価値 - 帳簿価額 = $1,500,000 - $900,000 = $600,000。この利益は損益に認識する。

questions.subtopic: Equity Transaction - Ownership Change Without Loss of Control
questions.stem: Parent owns 80% of Sub. The carrying amount of Sub's total equity (including NCI) is $2,000,000, so NCI = $400,000. Parent purchases an additional 10% from NCI holders for $250,000. What is the adjustment to Parent's additional paid-in capital (APIC)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $50,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase of $50,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease of $250,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment; goodwill increases by $50,000

questions.explanation: When a parent purchases additional shares without losing control, it is an equity transaction (no gain/loss). NCI reduction = 10%/20% × $400,000 = $200,000. Parent paid $250,000 for NCI with book value of $200,000. The excess $250,000 − $200,000 = $50,000 reduces Parent's APIC (or retained earnings if APIC is insufficient).
questions.explanationJa: 支配を維持したまま追加取得する場合は資本取引（損益なし）として処理する。NCI減少額 = 10%/20% × $400,000 = $200,000。親会社は帳簿価額$200,000のNCIに$250,000を支払った。超過額 $250,000 - $200,000 = $50,000は親会社のAPIC（不足なら利益剰余金）を減少させる。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Parent sold equipment to Sub for $360,000. The equipment had a carrying value of $300,000 on Parent's books (original cost $500,000, accumulated depreciation $200,000). Sub depreciates the equipment over 6 years straight-line with no residual value. What is the net consolidation adjustment to depreciation expense for Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease depreciation by $10,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase depreciation by $10,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease depreciation by $60,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment needed

questions.explanation: Sub records depreciation based on transfer price: $360,000 ÷ 6 = $60,000/year. From the consolidated perspective, the correct depreciation is based on original CV: $300,000 ÷ 6 = $50,000/year. Consolidation adjustment: Reduce depreciation by $60,000 − $50,000 = $10,000.
questions.explanationJa: 子会社は譲渡価格ベースで減価償却：$360,000 / 6 = $60,000/年。連結上の正しい減価償却は帳簿価額ベース：$300,000 / 6 = $50,000/年。連結修正で減価償却費を$60,000 - $50,000 = $10,000減少させる。

questions.stem: Parent owns 100% of Sub (carrying amount of Sub's net assets on consolidated books = $1,800,000). Parent sells 70% of Sub for $1,680,000 cash, retaining 30%. The fair value of the retained 30% interest is $720,000. What gain or loss does Parent recognize on deconsolidation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $600,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $480,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $120,000

questions.explanation: Upon loss of control, total gain = (Proceeds + FV of retained interest) − Carrying amount of subsidiary's net assets. Gain = ($1,680,000 + $720,000) − $1,800,000 = $2,400,000 − $1,800,000 = $600,000.

questions.stem: Parent acquires 100% of Target for $3,000,000 cash plus contingent consideration with an acquisition-date fair value of $400,000. Fair value of Target's identifiable net assets is $2,800,000. Two years later, the contingent consideration is settled for $500,000. What is the effect on Year 2 earnings from the contingent consideration settlement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase goodwill by $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $500,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect on earnings; adjust equity

questions.explanation: At acquisition, goodwill = ($3,000,000 + $400,000) − $2,800,000 = $600,000. Under ASC 805-30-35-1, changes in the fair value of contingent consideration classified as a liability after the acquisition date are recognized in earnings. The contingent consideration liability increased from $400,000 to $500,000, resulting in a $100,000 loss in Year 2 earnings. Goodwill is not adjusted.
questions.explanationJa: 取得時ののれん =（$3,000,000 + $400,000）- $2,800,000 = $600,000。取得日後、負債分類の条件付対価の公正価値変動は損益で認識する。条件付対価負債が$400,000から$500,000に増加したため、Year 2に$100,000の損失を計上する。のれんは修正しない。

questions.stem: Parent Company A acquires Subsidiary B on March 1, Year 1. At the acquisition date, Parent A provisionally estimates a contingent liability at $200,000. During the measurement period (within 12 months of acquisition), Parent A obtains new information that the liability should have been $280,000. How should the $80,000 adjustment be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a current period expense in Year 2

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a retrospective adjustment to the acquisition-date amounts, with corresponding adjustment to goodwill

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an adjustment to other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a gain since the liability was underestimated

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-17, measurement period adjustments are recognized as if the accounting for the business combination had been completed at the acquisition date. The $80,000 increase in the contingent liability is offset by a corresponding increase in goodwill (or bargain purchase gain). Comparative financial statements are adjusted accordingly.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-17により、測定期間の調整は企業結合の会計処理が取得日に完了していたかのように認識する。偶発負債の$80,000増加はのれん（またはバーゲン・パーチェス利得）の対応する調整で相殺される。比較財務諸表もそれに応じて修正する。

questions.stem: In a business combination, which of the following intangible assets must be recognized separately from goodwill?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only intangible assets currently being used by the acquiree

choices.label: B
choices.text: Intangible assets that meet the contractual-legal criterion or the separability criterion

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only intangible assets with a finite useful life

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only intangible assets that have been recorded on the acquiree's books

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-55-2, intangible assets are recognized separately from goodwill if they meet either the contractual-legal criterion (arise from contractual or legal rights) or the separability criterion (can be separated and sold, transferred, or licensed). This includes assets not previously recognized by the acquiree, such as customer relationships and trade names.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-55-2により、無形資産は契約上・法律上の基準（契約上・法律上の権利に基づく）または分離可能性の基準（分離して売却、移転、ライセンス供与できる）のいずれかを満たせば、のれんとは別に認識する。顧客関係や商号など被取得企業が以前認識していなかった資産も含む。

questions.stem: In a business combination, the acquirer identifies an in-process research and development (IPR&D) project with a fair value of $2,000,000. How should the acquirer account for this asset after the acquisition date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expense immediately as research and development cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalize as an indefinite-lived intangible asset until the project is completed or abandoned

choices.label: C
choices.text: Amortize over the expected project life beginning at the acquisition date

choices.label: D
choices.text: Include in goodwill

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-35-1, IPR&D acquired in a business combination is initially recognized at fair value as an indefinite-lived intangible asset. It is not amortized but tested for impairment under ASC 350 until the project is completed (at which point it becomes finite-lived and is amortized) or abandoned (written off).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-35-1により、企業結合で取得した仕掛研究開発は公正価値で耐用年数不確定の無形資産として当初認識する。プロジェクトが完了するまで（完了後は確定耐用年数として償却）または中止されるまで（除却）、償却せずASC 350に基づき減損テストを行う。

questions.stem: Before an acquisition, Parent C had a supply contract with Target D that was unfavorable to Parent C compared to market terms. At acquisition, the contract has a remaining term of 3 years and an off-market value of $150,000 (unfavorable to acquirer). How is this preexisting relationship accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included as part of the consideration transferred

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized as a settlement gain or loss separate from the business combination

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included in goodwill

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ignored because it existed before the acquisition

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-20, the settlement of a preexisting relationship between acquirer and acquiree is accounted for separately from the business combination. The settlement amount is measured based on the lesser of the off-market amount or any stated settlement provisions. A gain or loss is recognized in the income statement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-20により、取得者と被取得者間の既存関係の決済は企業結合とは別に会計処理する。決済額は市場外金額と契約上の決済条項のいずれか少ない方に基づき測定する。損益は損益計算書で認識する。

questions.stem: Parent P issued bonds with a carrying amount of $1,050,000 (including premium). Subsidiary S purchases the bonds on the open market for $980,000. In the consolidated financial statements, what is the effect of this intercompany bond holding?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A constructive gain of $70,000 is recognized in the consolidated income statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: A constructive loss of $70,000 is recognized

choices.label: C
choices.text: No gain or loss; the bond is simply eliminated

choices.label: D
choices.text: A gain of $1,050,000 is recognized

questions.explanation: When a subsidiary acquires an affiliate's bonds from the market, the bonds are considered constructively retired in consolidation. The difference between the issuer's carrying amount ($1,050,000) and the affiliate's purchase price ($980,000) results in a constructive gain of $70,000. This gain is recognized in the consolidated income statement in the year of the constructive retirement.
questions.explanationJa: 子会社が関連会社の社債を市場で取得すると、連結上、社債は推定的に償還されたとみなされる。発行者の帳簿価額($1,050,000)と関連会社の取得価額($980,000)の差額が推定利得$70,000となる。この利得は推定的償還の年に連結損益計算書で認識する。

questions.stem: Parent E acquires 100% of Subsidiary F for $5,000,000 cash. At acquisition, Subsidiary F has cash of $800,000. What amount should be reported as a cash outflow from investing activities in the consolidated statement of cash flows?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21, the cash paid for an acquisition is reported as an investing activity, net of cash acquired. Cash outflow = $5,000,000 − $800,000 = $4,200,000. A supplemental disclosure of the noncash assets and liabilities acquired is also required.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21により、企業取得に支払った現金は、取得した現金を控除して投資活動として報告する。現金流出 = $5,000,000 − $800,000 = $4,200,000。取得した非現金資産・負債の補足開示も必要である。

questions.stem: Entity G has a variable interest in Entity H (a VIE). Entity G has the power to direct the activities that most significantly affect Entity H's economic performance and has the obligation to absorb 60% of Entity H's expected losses. Is Entity G the primary beneficiary?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because Entity G has both power and the obligation to absorb losses or receive benefits that could be significant

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because Entity G must absorb all losses to be the primary beneficiary

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only if Entity G also has voting control

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because VIEs are not consolidated

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-25-38A, the primary beneficiary of a VIE is the entity that has (1) the power to direct the activities that most significantly affect the VIE's economic performance and (2) the obligation to absorb losses or the right to receive benefits that could potentially be significant to the VIE. Entity G meets both criteria.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-25-38Aにより、VIEの主たる受益者は(1) VIEの経済的業績に最も重要な影響を与える活動を指揮する力と(2) VIEにとって潜在的に重要な損失を負担する義務または利益を受ける権利の両方を有する企業である。Entity Gは両基準を満たす。

questions.subtopic: Partial Disposal Without Loss of Control
questions.stem: Parent J owns 80% of Subsidiary K. Parent J sells 10% of its interest to an outside party for $500,000. The carrying amount of the 10% NCI being created is $400,000. Parent J retains control with 70% ownership. How should this transaction be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a gain of $100,000 in the income statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record the $100,000 difference as an increase to Parent J's additional paid-in capital

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reduce goodwill by $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a loss of $100,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-23, when a parent sells a portion of its ownership interest but retains control, the transaction is accounted for as an equity transaction. No gain or loss is recognized in income. The difference between the proceeds ($500,000) and the carrying amount of the NCI created ($400,000) is recorded in additional paid-in capital ($100,000 increase).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-23により、親会社が支配を維持しながら持分の一部を売却する場合、資本取引として処理する。損益は認識しない。受取対価($500,000)と創出されるNCIの帳簿価額($400,000)の差額は追加払込資本の増加($100,000)として計上する。

questions.stem: Subsidiary L has outstanding cumulative preferred stock held by outside investors. The preferred stock pays annual dividends of $50,000. In the consolidated financial statements, how should the preferred stock dividends be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Eliminated in consolidation since it is an intercompany transaction

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deducted from consolidated net income attributable to the parent when computing the parent's share of subsidiary income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included as an expense in the consolidated income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: Added to consolidated revenue

questions.explanation: Subsidiary preferred stock held by outside parties is part of the noncontrolling interest. Preferred dividends must be deducted from the subsidiary's income before allocating income between the parent and NCI. The preferred dividends reduce the income attributable to common shareholders (both parent and NCI common).
questions.explanationJa: 外部投資家が保有する子会社優先株は非支配持分の一部である。親会社とNCIの間で利益を配分する前に、優先配当を子会社利益から控除しなければならない。優先配当は普通株主（親会社とNCI普通株の両方）に帰属する利益を減少させる。

questions.stem: Parent M acquired Subsidiary N for $10,000,000 when the fair value of N's identifiable net assets was $8,000,000, resulting in goodwill of $2,000,000. NCI at acquisition was $1,500,000 (fair value). In Year 3, the reporting unit's carrying amount is $12,000,000 and its fair value is $10,500,000. What is the goodwill impairment loss?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-20-35-3C (post-ASU 2017-04), goodwill impairment = Carrying amount of reporting unit − Fair value of reporting unit = $12,000,000 − $10,500,000 = $1,500,000. The impairment loss cannot exceed the carrying amount of goodwill ($2,000,000). Since $1,500,000 < $2,000,000, the full $1,500,000 is recognized.

questions.stem: Parent P owns 80% of Subsidiary Q, and Subsidiary Q owns 70% of Subsidiary R. What is the noncontrolling interest percentage in Subsidiary R from the consolidated perspective of Parent P?
questions.explanation: Parent P's effective ownership in R = 80% × 70% = 56%. Therefore, the NCI in R from Parent P's consolidated perspective = 100% − 56% = 44%. This includes both the direct NCI in R (30% held by outsiders) and the indirect NCI through Q's NCI holders (20% × 70% = 14%). Total NCI = 30% + 14% = 44%.
questions.explanationJa: Parent PのRに対する実効所有率 = 80% × 70% = 56%。従って、Parent Pの連結上のRのNCI = 100% − 56% = 44%。これはRの直接NCI（外部保有30%）と、QのNCI保有者を通じた間接NCI（20% × 70% = 14%）を含む。合計NCI = 30% + 14% = 44%。

questions.stem: Under ASC 805, when measuring noncontrolling interest at acquisition, which of the following options is available?

choices.label: A
choices.text: NCI must always be measured at fair value (full goodwill method)

choices.label: B
choices.text: NCI may be measured at fair value or at the NCI's proportionate share of identifiable net assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: NCI must always be measured at the proportionate share of identifiable net assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: NCI is measured at the book value of the subsidiary's equity

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-30-1, the acquirer may elect, on a transaction-by-transaction basis, to measure NCI either at fair value (full goodwill method) or at the NCI's proportionate share of the acquiree's identifiable net assets (partial goodwill method). The fair value method results in higher goodwill because goodwill attributable to NCI is also recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-30-1により、取得者は取引ごとに、NCIを公正価値（全額のれん法）またはNCIの被取得企業の識別可能純資産に対する比例持分（部分のれん法）のいずれかで測定する選択ができる。公正価値法ではNCIに帰属するのれんも認識されるため、のれんが大きくなる。

questions.stem: Parent S provides management services to Subsidiary T and charges $300,000 annually. In the consolidated financial statements, how should this intercompany service revenue be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Report the $300,000 as revenue and the corresponding expense in the consolidated statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Eliminate both the $300,000 revenue and the $300,000 expense in consolidation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Eliminate only the revenue; retain the expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Report the revenue but reclassify it as other income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-1, all intercompany transactions must be eliminated in consolidation. The management fee revenue recorded by the parent and the corresponding expense recorded by the subsidiary are both eliminated. From the consolidated entity's perspective, these are internal transfers with no economic substance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-1により、全てのグループ間取引は連結上消去しなければならない。親会社が計上した管理報酬収益と子会社が計上した対応する費用の両方を消去する。連結企業体の観点からは、これらは経済的実質のない内部振替である。

questions.stem: Parent U has net income of $2,000,000 and 500,000 common shares outstanding. Parent U owns 80% of Subsidiary V, which has net income of $500,000 and no dilutive securities. What is the consolidated basic EPS attributable to Parent U?
questions.explanation: Consolidated net income = Parent income + 100% of Subsidiary income = $2,000,000 + $500,000 = $2,500,000. NCI share = 20% × $500,000 = $100,000. Net income attributable to Parent = $2,500,000 − $100,000 = $2,400,000. Basic EPS = $2,400,000 / 500,000 shares = $4.80.

questions.stem: Parent W has a foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is the euro. When translating the subsidiary's financial statements for consolidation, which exchange rate is used for the subsidiary's common stock?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Current rate at the balance sheet date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Historical rate at the date of the subsidiary's stock issuance or acquisition

choices.label: C
choices.text: Average rate for the period

choices.label: D
choices.text: Forward rate

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-5, when translating foreign currency financial statements using the current rate method, equity accounts (common stock, APIC) are translated at historical rates — the rates in effect when the equity transactions occurred. Assets and liabilities use the current rate, and income statement items use the average rate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 830-10-45-5により、カレントレート法で外貨財務諸表を換算する場合、資本勘定（普通株式、追加払込資本）は歴史的レート（資本取引が発生した時点の為替レート）で換算する。資産・負債は決算日レート、損益計算書項目は期中平均レートを使用する。

questions.stem: Entity X acquires 100% of Entity Y. Entity Y elects to apply push-down accounting. Under ASC 805-50, what is the effect on Entity Y's separate financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entity Y restates its assets and liabilities to fair value and recognizes goodwill in its own financial statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entity Y continues to report at historical cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Entity Y records the fair value adjustments in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: Entity Y eliminates all equity and replaces it with Parent X's equity

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-50-30-1, when push-down accounting is elected, the acquiree adjusts its own separate financial statements to reflect the acquirer's new basis of accounting (acquisition-date fair values). This includes recognizing goodwill and fair value step-ups in the subsidiary's own books. Push-down accounting is an election, not a requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-50-30-1により、プッシュダウン会計を選択した場合、被取得企業は自身の個別財務諸表を取得者の新しい会計基準（取得日の公正価値）を反映するよう調整する。子会社自身の帳簿にのれんと公正価値ステップアップを認識することを含む。プッシュダウン会計は選択であり要求ではない。

questions.stem: Parent Z owns 70% of Subsidiary W. Parent Z acquires the remaining 30% NCI for $900,000. The carrying amount of the 30% NCI on the consolidated balance sheet is $750,000. How should Parent Z account for this acquisition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $150,000 loss in the income statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record $150,000 as goodwill

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reduce additional paid-in capital by $150,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a $150,000 gain in the income statement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-23, the acquisition of NCI when the parent already controls the subsidiary is an equity transaction. No gain or loss is recognized. The difference between consideration paid ($900,000) and the carrying amount of NCI ($750,000) is $150,000, which is recorded as a reduction of APIC (or retained earnings if APIC is insufficient).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-23により、親会社が既に支配している子会社のNCIの取得は資本取引である。損益は認識しない。支払対価($900,000)とNCIの帳簿価額($750,000)の差額$150,000は追加払込資本の減額（APICが不足する場合は利益剰余金の減額）として計上する。

questions.stem: Which of the following would be considered a variable interest in an entity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A fixed-rate, fully collateralized loan to the entity

choices.label: B
choices.text: A guarantee of the entity's debt

choices.label: C
choices.text: A normal purchase order for goods at market prices

choices.label: D
choices.text: A fixed-fee service contract at market rates

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-25-22, variable interests absorb portions of an entity's expected losses or receive portions of its expected residual returns. A debt guarantee absorbs losses if the entity defaults, making it a variable interest. Fixed-rate collateralized loans (where credit risk is mitigated), normal purchase orders, and fixed-fee service contracts at market rates generally are not variable interests.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-25-22により、変動持分は企業の期待損失の一部を吸収するか、期待残余利益の一部を受け取る。債務保証は企業がデフォルトした場合に損失を吸収するため、変動持分に該当する。担保付固定金利ローン、通常の購買発注、市場価格の固定報酬サービス契約は一般に変動持分に該当しない。

questions.stem: In a business combination, the acquirer identifies a highly skilled assembled workforce at the acquiree. Can the assembled workforce be recognized as a separate intangible asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, if the fair value can be reliably measured

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, it is subsumed into goodwill because it does not meet the separability or contractual-legal criterion

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, as an indefinite-lived intangible asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, but only if the employees have non-compete agreements

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-55-6, an assembled workforce is generally not recognized as a separate intangible asset because it typically does not meet the contractual-legal criterion or the separability criterion. The value of the assembled workforce is subsumed into goodwill. However, non-compete agreements with employees are separately recognizable intangible assets.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-55-6により、編成された労働力は通常、契約上・法律上の基準または分離可能性の基準を満たさないため、個別の無形資産として認識されない。その価値はのれんに含まれる。ただし、従業員との競業禁止契約は個別に認識可能な無形資産である。

questions.stem: Subsidiary A owns 5% of the outstanding shares of its Parent B. How should this reciprocal shareholding be treated in the consolidated financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Report the subsidiary's investment in the parent as an asset on the consolidated balance sheet

choices.label: B
choices.text: Treat the parent shares held by the subsidiary as treasury stock in the consolidated financial statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: Eliminate the investment and recognize a gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: Report the shares as an available-for-sale investment

questions.explanation: When a subsidiary holds shares of its parent company, the consolidated financial statements treat those shares as treasury stock. The cost of the parent's shares held by the subsidiary is deducted from consolidated equity, consistent with the treatment of any other treasury stock. No gain or loss is recognized on the parent's shares held by the subsidiary.
questions.explanationJa: 子会社が親会社の株式を保有する場合、連結財務諸表ではそれらの株式を自己株式として扱う。子会社が保有する親会社株式の取得原価は連結資本から控除され、他の自己株式と同様の処理となる。子会社が保有する親会社株式について損益は認識しない。

questions.stem: Parent acquires 70% of Sub for $7,000,000. Sub's identifiable net assets have a fair value of $8,000,000. The fair value of the 30% NCI is $3,200,000. Under U.S. GAAP (full goodwill method), what is goodwill?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-30-1 (full goodwill method required by U.S. GAAP), Goodwill = (Consideration + NCI fair value) − Fair value of net assets = ($7,000,000 + $3,200,000) − $8,000,000 = $2,200,000. Under the partial goodwill method (permitted under IFRS), goodwill would be only $7,000,000 − (70% × $8,000,000) = $1,400,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-30-1（U.S. GAAP必須の全額のれん法）により、のれん = (移転対価 + NCI公正価値) − 純資産公正価値 = ($7,000,000 + $3,200,000) − $8,000,000 = $2,200,000。IFRS部分のれん法では$1,400,000。

questions.stem: How should the noncontrolling interest (NCI) be presented on the consolidated balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In the equity section, separately from the parent's equity

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: As mezzanine equity between liabilities and equity

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a deduction from consolidated assets

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-16, the noncontrolling interest is reported in the equity section of the consolidated balance sheet, separately from the parent's equity. It is labeled clearly as 'noncontrolling interest' or 'minority interest.' NCI is not a liability because it represents ownership interests, not obligations. The requirement for equity classification was established by SFAS 160 (now ASC 810).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-16により、非支配持分は連結BS の資本の部に、親会社の資本とは別に表示する。負債ではなく所有持分を表すため資本に分類。SFAS 160（現ASC 810）で資本分類が確立。

questions.stem: Parent sells equipment (carrying amount $200,000) to its 80%-owned Subsidiary for $300,000 on January 1, Year 1. Subsidiary depreciates it over 5 years, straight-line. What consolidation elimination entry is needed at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Eliminate $100,000 gain and adjust depreciation by $20,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Eliminate $80,000 gain (parent's 80% share only)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Eliminate $100,000 gain only; no depreciation adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: No elimination needed for downstream sales

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-1, all intercompany profits (both upstream and downstream) must be fully eliminated in consolidation. The $100,000 gain on the sale is eliminated. The subsidiary is depreciating based on $300,000 (excess depreciation = $100,000/5 = $20,000/year). The depreciation adjustment restores $20,000 in Year 1, reducing the unrealized gain to $80,000 at year-end. NCI allocation is not needed for downstream sales — the full elimination is against the parent's income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-1により、会社間利益は全額消去する。$100,000の売却益を消去。子会社は$300,000で減価償却（超過額 = $20,000/年）。Year 1に$20,000の減価償却調整で未実現利益は$80,000に減少。下流取引のため親会社の利益に対して全額消去。

questions.stem: Entity A holds a variable interest in Entity B (a VIE). Entity A has the power to direct the activities that most significantly impact Entity B's economic performance. However, Entity C (an unrelated party) holds kick-out rights that allow it to remove Entity A as the decision-maker. Are these kick-out rights substantive?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, if the rights are exercisable by a single party without cause

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, kick-out rights are never considered substantive

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, only if exercisable by a majority of related parties

choices.label: D
choices.text: Substantiveness is irrelevant in VIE analysis

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-25-38D, kick-out rights are considered in determining the primary beneficiary of a VIE. Kick-out rights are substantive if they are exercisable by a single party without cause (i.e., the holder can remove the decision-maker unilaterally without requiring a reason). If Entity C has substantive kick-out rights, Entity A may not be the primary beneficiary despite having power, because the power can be removed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-25-38Dにより、キックアウト権はVIEの主たる受益者の判定で考慮される。単一の当事者が理由なく行使できる場合（一方的に意思決定者を解任可能）、キックアウト権は実質的。実質的キックアウト権がある場合、Entity Aは主たる受益者でない可能性がある。

questions.stem: Acquirer pays $3,000,000 for 100% of Target. The fair value of Target's identifiable net assets is $3,500,000. The NCI is $0 (100% acquired). What is the accounting treatment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $500,000 gain from bargain purchase in the income statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record negative goodwill of $500,000 on the balance sheet

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reduce the fair values of noncurrent assets proportionally

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer the $500,000 and amortize over 10 years

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-30-25-2, if the fair value of identifiable net assets exceeds the consideration transferred (plus NCI and previously held equity interests), the acquirer first reassesses whether all assets and liabilities have been properly identified and measured. If the excess remains after reassessment, the acquirer recognizes the excess as a gain (bargain purchase gain) in earnings on the acquisition date.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-30-25-2により、識別可能純資産の公正価値が移転対価を超過する場合、まず全ての資産・負債の識別と測定を再評価。超過が残る場合、取得日の損益に利得（バーゲンパーチェス利得）$500,000を認識。負ののれんのBS計上や繰延は認められない。

questions.stem: Parent Co. issued bonds at par ($1,000,000). Subsidiary purchases $400,000 face value of these bonds on the open market for $380,000. In the consolidation, what elimination entry is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Eliminate $400,000 of bonds payable and the $380,000 investment, recognizing a $20,000 gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: Eliminate $380,000 of bonds payable and the $380,000 investment

choices.label: C
choices.text: No elimination because the subsidiary purchased on the open market

choices.label: D
choices.text: Eliminate $400,000 of bonds payable and recognize a $20,000 loss

questions.explanation: When a subsidiary purchases the parent's bonds on the open market, the bonds are effectively retired from the consolidated entity's perspective. The consolidation eliminates the $400,000 bonds payable (parent's books) against the $380,000 investment in bonds (subsidiary's books). The $20,000 difference is recognized as a constructive gain on retirement of debt in the consolidated income statement.
questions.explanationJa: 子会社が親会社の社債を市場で購入した場合、連結上は社債が実質的に消却される。社債$400,000（親）と投資$380,000（子）を消去。差額$20,000を連結損益計算書で建設的消却利得として認識。

questions.stem: Company P and Company S sign a merger agreement on March 15. Regulatory approval is received on May 1. The transaction closes and control transfers on June 1. What is the acquisition date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: March 15 (agreement date)

choices.label: B
choices.text: May 1 (regulatory approval date)

choices.label: C
choices.text: June 1 (closing date when control is obtained)

choices.label: D
choices.text: The earliest of the three dates

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-6, the acquisition date is the date on which the acquirer obtains control of the acquiree. This is generally the closing date when the consideration is transferred, assets are acquired, and liabilities are assumed. The agreement date and regulatory approval date are not the acquisition date because control has not yet transferred. All measurements (fair values, goodwill) are determined as of the acquisition date.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-6により、取得日は取得企業が被取得企業の支配を獲得した日。通常はクロージング日（対価移転、資産取得、負債引受の日）。合意日や規制承認日は支配移転前のため取得日ではない。全ての測定は取得日時点で行う。

questions.stem: Parent owns 80% of Sub. Consolidated net income before NCI allocation is $1,000,000. Sub's net income is $300,000 (already included). What is net income attributable to the parent?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-20, consolidated net income is allocated between the parent and the NCI based on ownership percentages. NCI's share = $300,000 × 20% = $60,000. Net income attributable to parent = $1,000,000 − $60,000 = $940,000. The consolidated income statement shows total net income of $1,000,000 with a deduction for NCI of $60,000.

questions.stem: Company A acquires 100% of Company B. Under ASC 805-50, when may Company B elect to apply push-down accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When a change-in-control event occurs (an entity obtains control)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only when the acquirer owns more than 90% of the target

choices.label: C
choices.text: Push-down accounting is always required, not elective

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only for SEC registrants

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-50-25-4 (ASU 2014-17), an acquired entity may elect to apply push-down accounting in its separate financial statements when a change-in-control event occurs. The election is available regardless of whether the acquired entity is a public or nonpublic entity. The election can be made at the time of the change-in-control event or in a subsequent period. It is an election, not a requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-50-25-4（ASU 2014-17）により、支配の変更が発生した場合、被取得企業は個別財務諸表でプッシュダウン会計を選択適用できる。公開・非公開企業を問わず利用可能。支配変更時またはその後の期間に選択可能。要件ではなく選択。

questions.stem: Under ASC 350, at what level is goodwill tested for impairment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the reporting unit level

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the consolidated entity level

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the individual asset level

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the legal entity level

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-20-35-33, goodwill is tested for impairment at the reporting unit level. A reporting unit is an operating segment or one level below an operating segment. Goodwill is assigned to reporting units at the acquisition date based on which reporting units are expected to benefit from the synergies of the business combination. The impairment test compares the fair value of the reporting unit to its carrying amount (including goodwill).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-20-35-33により、のれんは報告単位レベルで減損テストを行う。報告単位は事業セグメントまたはその1つ下のレベル。のれんは企業結合のシナジーから便益を受ける報告単位に取得日に配分。減損テストは報告単位の公正価値と帳簿価額を比較。

questions.stem: After a business combination, Acquirer Corp. discovers additional information about a preexisting contingent liability of the acquiree. The acquisition occurred 14 months ago. Can Acquirer adjust the provisional amounts?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, because the measurement period cannot exceed one year from the acquisition date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, because the measurement period has no maximum limit

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but the adjustment is recognized prospectively

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because contingent liabilities cannot be adjusted

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-17, the measurement period is the period after the acquisition date during which the acquirer may adjust provisional amounts recognized at the acquisition date. The measurement period ends as soon as the acquirer receives the information needed, but shall not exceed one year from the acquisition date. Since 14 months have passed, the measurement period has ended and no retroactive adjustment can be made. Any changes must be recognized in current-period earnings.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-17により、測定期間は取得日から最長1年。14ヶ月経過しているため測定期間は終了し、遡及調整は不可。変更は当期の損益で認識する。

questions.stem: Parent owns 80% of Sub and sells 50% (reducing ownership to 30%). Parent loses control. At the date of sale, Sub's net assets have a carrying amount of $5,000,000. The fair value of the 30% retained investment is $1,800,000. The sale price for the 50% is $3,000,000. What gain does Parent recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-40-5, when a parent loses control, it derecognizes all of the subsidiary's assets and liabilities, recognizes the fair value of the retained investment and any consideration received, and recognizes a gain or loss. Gain = Sale proceeds + FV of retained investment − Carrying amount of former subsidiary's net assets − NCI carrying amount. Parent's share of net assets = 80% × $5,000,000 = $4,000,000. Gain = $3,000,000 + $1,800,000 − $4,000,000 = $800,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-40-5により、支配喪失時に子会社の全資産・負債を認識中止し、残存投資の公正価値と受取対価を認識。利得 = 売却代金$3,000,000 + 残存投資FV$1,800,000 − 親会社持分$4,000,000 = $800,000。

questions.stem: In Year 1, Sub (80%-owned) sold inventory to Parent for $500,000 (cost to Sub: $350,000). By December 31, Year 1, Parent had not resold the inventory. In Year 2, Parent sells all the inventory to outside parties. What consolidation adjustment is needed in Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease beginning retained earnings by $150,000 and increase cost of goods sold by $150,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease beginning retained earnings by $120,000 (Parent's 80% share) and adjust COGS

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease beginning retained earnings by $150,000 and decrease cost of goods sold by $150,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment needed in Year 2

questions.explanation: In Year 2, the unrealized profit from Year 1 ($150,000) is realized because the inventory was sold to outside parties. The consolidation entry adjusts the opening balance: Debit Beginning Retained Earnings $120,000 (Parent's share of $150,000 × 80%), Debit NCI $30,000 (20% share — upstream sale), Credit COGS $150,000. This effectively decreases COGS (realizing the previously deferred profit) and adjusts opening equity for the prior-year elimination.
questions.explanationJa: Year 2に在庫が外部に販売され、Year 1の未実現利益$150,000が実現。連結仕訳：期首RE$120,000借方（親80%分）、NCI$30,000借方（20%分—上流取引）、売上原価$150,000貸方。以前繰延した利益を実現させる。

questions.stem: Under the voting interest model, what level of ownership generally requires consolidation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: More than 50% of the outstanding voting interests

choices.label: B
choices.text: 20% or more of the outstanding voting interests

choices.label: C
choices.text: Any ownership interest if significant influence exists

choices.label: D
choices.text: More than 25% of the outstanding voting interests

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15-8, the voting interest model requires consolidation when one entity owns more than 50% of the outstanding voting interests of another entity, giving it a controlling financial interest. Ownership of 20-50% typically indicates significant influence (equity method), while less than 20% generally means no significant influence. The 50% threshold can be overcome in rare circumstances where control exists without majority ownership.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-15-8により、議決権モデルでは、議決権の50%超を所有する場合に連結が必要。支配的財務持分を有する。20-50%は通常重要な影響力（持分法）。50%以下でも支配がある稀なケースは例外。

questions.stem: Acquirer Corp. incurs $500,000 in legal fees and $200,000 in finder's fees directly related to a business combination. How should these costs be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expensed as incurred in the periods in which the costs are incurred

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalized as part of the cost of the acquisition (added to goodwill)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Charged directly to retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortized over the expected benefit period

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-23, acquisition-related costs (such as finder's fees, advisory fees, legal fees, accounting fees, valuation fees, and other professional or consulting fees) are costs that the acquirer incurs to effect a business combination. These costs are expensed in the periods in which they are incurred and the services are received. They are NOT capitalized as part of the business combination. The only exception is costs to issue debt or equity securities, which are accounted for under ASC 470 or ASC 505.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-23により、企業結合に関連する取得費用（法務、助言、ファインダーズフィー等）は発生期間に費用処理する。企業結合の一部として資本化しない。例外は債務・持分証券の発行費用のみ（ASC 470/505に従う）。

questions.subtopic: Ownership Change Without Loss of Control
questions.stem: Parent owns 80% of Sub. Parent purchases an additional 10% from NCI for $1,500,000. The carrying amount of the 10% NCI on the consolidated balance sheet is $1,200,000. How is this transaction recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease NCI by $1,200,000, decrease APIC by $300,000, debit cash $1,500,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase goodwill by $300,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a loss of $300,000 in the income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease NCI by $1,500,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-23, when a parent acquires additional ownership in a subsidiary without a change in control, the transaction is accounted for as an equity transaction. The difference between the consideration paid ($1,500,000) and the carrying amount of the NCI acquired ($1,200,000) = $300,000 is charged to the parent's equity (typically APIC or retained earnings). No gain, loss, or goodwill adjustment is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-23により、支配の変更なく追加持分を取得する場合は資本取引として処理。対価$1,500,000とNCI帳簿価額$1,200,000の差額$300,000は親会社の資本（APIC等）から控除。損益やのれん調整は認識しない。

questions.subtopic: Consolidation of Sub with Different Year-End
questions.stem: Parent has a December 31 fiscal year-end. Sub has a September 30 fiscal year-end. Under ASC 810, how should the consolidation be handled?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sub must prepare financial statements as of December 31 for consolidation purposes, or use its September 30 statements if the difference is 3 months or less with disclosure of significant intervening events

choices.label: B
choices.text: Sub's September 30 financial statements are always used without adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Consolidation is not possible when fiscal years differ

choices.label: D
choices.text: Parent must change its fiscal year to match the subsidiary

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-12, when a parent and subsidiary have different reporting periods, the subsidiary should prepare financial statements as of the parent's reporting date for consolidation. However, if the difference is three months or less, the subsidiary's existing financial statements may be used, provided that significant events occurring between the subsidiary's and parent's reporting dates are disclosed and recognized as appropriate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-12により、親子会社の報告期間が異なる場合、子会社は親会社の報告日の財務諸表を作成すべき。ただし差異が3ヶ月以内であれば、子会社の既存の財務諸表を使用可能。重要な中間事象の開示・認識が必要。

questions.stem: Sub has cumulative losses that cause the NCI balance to become negative (deficit). Under current GAAP, how are the subsidiary's losses allocated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Losses continue to be allocated to the NCI, even if it creates a deficit balance

choices.label: B
choices.text: Losses in excess of the NCI balance are allocated entirely to the parent

choices.label: C
choices.text: The subsidiary is deconsolidated when NCI reaches zero

choices.label: D
choices.text: Losses are allocated to the NCI only up to its positive balance

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-21, comprehensive income (including losses) is attributed to the parent and the NCI, even if that attribution results in a deficit NCI balance. This is a change from prior GAAP, which stopped allocating losses to NCI once the NCI balance reached zero. The current standard ensures that the NCI reflects its true proportionate share of the subsidiary's net assets (or deficit).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-21により、包括利益（損失を含む）はNCI残高が赤字になっても親会社とNCIに帰属させる。以前のGAAPではNCI残高がゼロになると損失配分を停止したが、現行基準ではNCIの真の比例持分を反映する。

questions.stem: Company A (legal acquirer) issues shares to acquire Company B (legal acquiree). However, former Company B shareholders hold 65% of the outstanding shares of the combined entity after the transaction. Under ASC 805, which entity is considered the acquirer for accounting purposes?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Company A, because it legally issued the shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: Company B, because its former shareholders control the combined entity

choices.label: C
choices.text: The combined entity is treated as a new entity

choices.label: D
choices.text: The determination depends on which company has the larger board of directors

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-55-11 through 55-15, in a reverse acquisition, the entity that issues securities (legal acquirer) is identified as the acquiree for accounting purposes. The accounting acquirer is the entity whose former owners as a group retain the largest portion of voting rights in the combined entity. Since Company B's former shareholders hold 65% of the combined entity, Company B is the accounting acquirer despite being the legal acquiree.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-55-11〜55-15により、逆取得では証券を発行した法的取得者が会計上の被取得者となる。会計上の取得者は、結合後の企業で最大の議決権を保持する旧株主グループの企業である。B社の旧株主が結合企業の65%を保有するため、B社が法的被取得者であっても会計上の取得者となる。

questions.stem: Parent Co. acquired 100% of Sub Co. on January 1, Year 1 for $500,000. At acquisition, Sub's retained earnings were $120,000. At December 31, Year 3, Parent's standalone retained earnings are $800,000, and Sub's retained earnings are $200,000. Sub paid $30,000 in dividends to Parent during Years 1-3. What is the consolidated retained earnings at December 31, Year 3?
questions.explanation: Consolidated retained earnings equals the parent's standalone retained earnings plus the parent's share of the subsidiary's post-acquisition change in retained earnings. Sub's post-acquisition increase in retained earnings = $200,000 - $120,000 = $80,000. Since Parent owns 100%, consolidated retained earnings = $800,000 + $80,000 = $880,000. The dividends from Sub to Parent are eliminated in consolidation and do not affect consolidated retained earnings (they are already reflected in Parent's standalone retained earnings under the equity method, or the calculation above captures the net change).
questions.explanationJa: 連結利益剰余金は、親会社の個別利益剰余金に子会社の取得後利益剰余金変動額の親会社持分を加算して算出する。子会社の取得後増加額＝$200,000−$120,000＝$80,000。親会社が100%保有のため、連結利益剰余金＝$800,000＋$80,000＝$880,000。子会社から親会社への配当は連結上消去され、連結利益剰余金に影響しない。

questions.stem: Parent owns 80% of Subsidiary. During the year, Subsidiary declared and paid $100,000 in dividends. What is the effect of the intercompany dividend elimination entry on the consolidated financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dividend income of $80,000 is eliminated and NCI dividends of $20,000 are reported as a financing activity

choices.label: B
choices.text: The full $100,000 dividend is eliminated from the consolidated statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: Dividend income of $100,000 is eliminated with no other effect

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only $20,000 paid to NCI shareholders is eliminated

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45, the parent's share of dividends ($100,000 x 80% = $80,000) is eliminated as an intercompany transaction. The NCI share ($100,000 x 20% = $20,000) is paid to outside shareholders and is not eliminated. In the consolidated statement of cash flows, the $20,000 NCI dividend is reported as a financing activity under ASC 230-10-45-15. This reflects that dividends to NCI shareholders are distributions to non-parent equity holders.
questions.explanationJa: 親会社持分の配当（$100,000×80%＝$80,000）は会社間取引として消去される。NCI持分（$100,000×20%＝$20,000）は外部株主への支払いであり消去されない。連結キャッシュ・フロー計算書では、NCIへの配当$20,000は財務活動として報告される。

questions.stem: Parent sold land to its 100%-owned Subsidiary for $350,000. Parent's carrying amount of the land was $200,000. At year-end, Subsidiary still holds the land. What elimination entry is required in the consolidated working papers?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Gain on Sale $150,000; Credit Land $150,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Land $150,000; Credit Gain on Sale $150,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Gain on Sale $150,000; Credit Retained Earnings $150,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No elimination is needed because the land was sold at fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-1, all intercompany profits must be eliminated in consolidation. Parent recorded a gain of $350,000 - $200,000 = $150,000 on the sale. Since the land is non-depreciable, the unrealized gain remains until the land is sold to an outside party. The elimination entry debits Gain on Sale for $150,000 (to remove the gain from the income statement) and credits Land for $150,000 (to reduce it from $350,000 back to the original $200,000 cost to the consolidated entity).
questions.explanationJa: 会社間利益は全て連結上消去する。親会社は$350,000−$200,000＝$150,000の売却益を計上した。土地は非償却資産のため、外部売却まで未実現利益が残る。消去仕訳：売却益$150,000を借方（損益計算書から除去）、土地$150,000を貸方（$350,000から連結上の原価$200,000に戻す）。

questions.stem: Subsidiary (80%-owned) sold equipment to Parent on January 1, Year 1 for $180,000. The equipment had a carrying value of $120,000 on Sub's books with a remaining useful life of 6 years (straight-line, no salvage). What is the net unrealized intercompany profit to be eliminated at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: The intercompany gain on the sale = $180,000 - $120,000 = $60,000. Parent depreciates the equipment based on $180,000 over 6 years = $30,000/year. The correct consolidated depreciation should be based on $120,000 / 6 = $20,000/year. The excess depreciation = $30,000 - $20,000 = $10,000 per year. This excess depreciation represents the portion of the intercompany gain that is realized through use during Year 1. Net unrealized profit at December 31, Year 1 = $60,000 - $10,000 = $50,000.

questions.stem: Parent sold inventory to its 80%-owned Subsidiary for $250,000. Parent's cost was $175,000. At year-end, 40% of this inventory remains unsold by Subsidiary. What amount of unrealized intercompany profit must be eliminated in the consolidated financial statements?
questions.explanation: Total intercompany profit = $250,000 - $175,000 = $75,000. Since 60% of the inventory has been sold to outside parties, that portion is realized. Only the profit on the remaining 40% inventory is unrealized. Unrealized profit = $75,000 x 40% = $30,000. For a downstream sale (parent to subsidiary), 100% of the unrealized profit is eliminated against the parent's income, regardless of the NCI percentage. The NCI share is not relevant for downstream eliminations.
questions.explanationJa: 会社間利益合計＝$250,000−$175,000＝$75,000。60%が外部販売済みで実現済み。残り40%の在庫の利益が未実現。未実現利益＝$75,000×40%＝$30,000。下流取引（親→子）では、NCI持分に関係なく未実現利益の100%を親会社の利益から消去する。

questions.stem: Which of the following items would NOT appear as an elimination entry on a consolidated balance sheet working paper?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Elimination of the parent's investment in subsidiary account against subsidiary's equity

choices.label: B
choices.text: Elimination of intercompany receivables and payables

choices.label: C
choices.text: Elimination of the subsidiary's external accounts receivable

choices.label: D
choices.text: Elimination of unrealized profit in intercompany inventory transfers

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-1, consolidation elimination entries remove intercompany balances and transactions, not external transactions. The subsidiary's external accounts receivable are from third parties and must be included in the consolidated balance sheet. The investment in subsidiary against subsidiary equity (A), intercompany receivables/payables (B), and unrealized intercompany inventory profit (D) are all standard elimination entries.
questions.explanationJa: 連結消去仕訳は会社間残高・取引を消去するものであり、外部取引は消去しない。子会社の外部売掛金は第三者に対するものであり、連結貸借対照表に含める。投資と子会社資本の消去（A）、会社間債権債務の消去（B）、会社間在庫未実現利益の消去（D）はいずれも標準的な消去仕訳である。

questions.subtopic: NCI Valuation at Fair Value vs Proportionate Share
questions.stem: Parent acquires 75% of Sub for $900,000 when Sub's identifiable net assets have a fair value of $1,000,000. If NCI is measured at fair value and the fair value of the 25% NCI is $320,000, what is the goodwill recognized in the consolidated financial statements?
questions.explanation: Under the full goodwill method (ASC 805-20-30-1), goodwill is calculated as the total implied value of the entity minus the fair value of identifiable net assets. Total implied value = Consideration transferred ($900,000) + Fair value of NCI ($320,000) = $1,220,000. Goodwill = $1,220,000 - $1,000,000 = $220,000. This method attributes goodwill to both the parent's and the NCI's portions.
questions.explanationJa: 全額のれん法（ASC 805-20-30-1）では、のれん＝企業の推定総価値−識別可能純資産の公正価値。推定総価値＝移転対価（$900,000）＋NCIの公正価値（$320,000）＝$1,220,000。のれん＝$1,220,000−$1,000,000＝$220,000。この方法では親会社分とNCI分の両方にのれんを帰属させる。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Parent acquired Sub and agreed to pay an additional $200,000 if Sub achieves a revenue target by December 31, Year 2. The acquisition-date fair value of the contingent consideration was $140,000, classified as a liability. At December 31, Year 1, the fair value of the contingent consideration increased to $175,000. How should the $35,000 change be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As an adjustment to goodwill

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a loss in earnings for Year 1

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an adjustment to additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a component of other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-30-35-1, changes in the fair value of contingent consideration classified as a liability that occur after the acquisition date (and outside the measurement period adjustment criteria) are recognized in earnings. Since this is a remeasurement at December 31, Year 1 (not a measurement period adjustment based on facts existing at the acquisition date), the $35,000 increase is recognized as a loss in earnings. Contingent consideration classified as equity is not remeasured.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-30-35-1により、負債に分類された条件付対価の取得日後の公正価値変動（測定期間調整に該当しない場合）は損益に認識する。これは取得日に存在した事実に基づく測定期間調整ではなく12月31日の再測定であるため、$35,000の増加は損益として認識する。資本に分類された条件付対価は再測定しない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 350, an entity may elect to perform a qualitative assessment before testing goodwill for impairment. Which of the following best describes the purpose of the qualitative assessment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To determine the exact amount of goodwill impairment loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: To assess whether it is more likely than not that the fair value of a reporting unit is less than its carrying amount

choices.label: C
choices.text: To compare the carrying amount of goodwill to its implied fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: To allocate goodwill to individual assets within the reporting unit

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-20-35-3A through 3G, an entity has the option to first perform a qualitative assessment (often called 'Step 0') to determine whether it is more likely than not (i.e., a likelihood of more than 50%) that the fair value of a reporting unit is less than its carrying amount. If the entity concludes it is not more likely than not, no further quantitative testing is required. This simplifies the impairment testing process for entities where goodwill impairment is clearly unlikely.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-20-35-3A〜3Gにより、企業は定性的評価（「ステップ0」）を先に実施し、報告単位の公正価値がその帳簿価額を下回る可能性が50%超か否かを判断できる。50%超でないと結論すれば、追加の定量テストは不要である。のれん減損が明らかに見込まれない企業にとって、減損テストプロセスを簡素化する。

questions.stem: Parent Co. distributes 100% of its ownership in Subsidiary to Parent's existing shareholders as a spin-off. Immediately before the spin-off, the carrying amount of the net assets of Subsidiary on the consolidated books is $400,000. How does the parent account for the spin-off?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record a gain or loss based on the fair value of Subsidiary

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reduce retained earnings by $400,000 (or equity if retained earnings is insufficient)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize the distribution at fair value and record any difference as a gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reclassify the investment to a held-for-sale asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 505-60-25, a nonreciprocal transfer of a subsidiary to owners in a spin-off is accounted for at the carrying amount (book value) of the subsidiary's net assets. No gain or loss is recognized. The parent reduces equity (typically retained earnings) by the carrying amount of the net assets distributed. This is because the transaction is with owners in their capacity as owners, similar to a dividend.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 505-60-25により、スピンオフにおける子会社の株主への非相互移転は、子会社の純資産の帳簿価額で会計処理する。損益は認識しない。親会社は分配した純資産の帳簿価額で資本（通常は利益剰余金）を減額する。これは所有者としての立場での所有者との取引であり、配当と同様の性質を持つためである。

questions.stem: Entity A owns 45% of Entity B's voting stock. Entity A also has a contractual arrangement that gives it the unilateral power to direct Entity B's key operating and financial activities. Under ASC 810, should Entity A consolidate Entity B?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, because Entity A does not own more than 50% of the voting stock

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, if Entity B is a variable interest entity and Entity A is the primary beneficiary

choices.label: C
choices.text: No, Entity A should use the equity method

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, only if Entity A also has the power to appoint the majority of the board

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15, the consolidation model includes both the voting interest model and the variable interest entity (VIE) model. Even without majority voting ownership, Entity A may be required to consolidate Entity B if Entity B is a VIE and Entity A is the primary beneficiary. The primary beneficiary is the entity with (1) power to direct the activities that most significantly impact the VIE's economic performance and (2) the obligation to absorb losses or the right to receive benefits that could potentially be significant to the VIE (ASC 810-10-25-38A).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-15により、連結モデルには議決権モデルとVIEモデルの両方が含まれる。過半数の議決権を保有していなくても、B社がVIEであり A社が主たる受益者であれば連結が必要となる。主たる受益者とは、(1)VIEの経済的業績に最も重要な影響を与える活動を指揮する力と、(2)VIEにとって重要となり得る損失負担義務または利益受領権を有する企業である。

questions.stem: Investor Corp. owns 40% of Target Inc. The remaining 60% is widely dispersed. The shareholder agreement gives the 60% holders the right to approve Target's annual operating budget and block asset sales exceeding $5 million, but not to unilaterally set operating or financing policies. These rights held by the 60% holders are best characterized as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Participating rights that overcome the presumption of control by Investor Corp.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Protective rights that do not overcome the presumption of control

choices.label: C
choices.text: Kick-out rights that require consolidation by the 60% holders

choices.label: D
choices.text: Substantive participating rights that prevent any party from consolidating

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-25-5, protective rights are those designed to protect the interest of the holder without giving that holder a controlling financial interest. Rights such as approving budgets or blocking large transactions are generally protective in nature. Participating rights, by contrast, allow the holder to participate in directing significant financial and operating decisions made in the ordinary course of business. Since these rights do not allow the 60% holders to unilaterally direct operating or financing policies, they are protective rights.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-25-5により、防御的権利は保有者の利益を保護するものであり、支配的財務持分を与えるものではない。予算承認や大規模取引の拒否権などは一般に防御的性質を持つ。参加的権利は、通常の事業運営における重要な財務・経営意思決定への参加を可能にする。60%保有者が経営・財務方針を一方的に決定できないため、これらは防御的権利である。

questions.stem: Two unrelated entities, Alpha and Beta, each have the power to direct different activities of a VIE. Alpha directs the lending decisions that most significantly impact the VIE's economic performance, while Beta directs the asset management activities. Under ASC 810-10-25-38D, which entity is the primary beneficiary?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Alpha, because it directs the most significant activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Beta, because asset management is more continuous

choices.label: C
choices.text: Neither entity consolidates if power is shared and no single party directs the most significant activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both entities must consolidate their proportionate share

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-25-38A and 38D, the primary beneficiary must have the power to direct the activities that most significantly impact the VIE's economic performance. When multiple parties hold power over different activities, the entity that directs the activities that are most significant to the VIE's economic performance is the primary beneficiary (assuming the other criterion regarding significant variable interests is also met). Since Alpha directs the lending decisions that most significantly impact economic performance, Alpha is the primary beneficiary.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-25-38A・38Dにより、主たる受益者はVIEの経済的業績に最も重要な影響を与える活動を指揮する力を持つ必要がある。複数当事者が異なる活動に対する力を持つ場合、VIEの経済的業績に最も重要な活動を指揮する企業が主たる受益者となる。Alphaが最も重要な影響を与える融資判断を指揮するため、Alphaが主たる受益者である。

questions.subtopic: VIE - Expected Losses and Expected Returns
questions.stem: Entity X holds a variable interest in Entity Y (a VIE). Entity X has the power to direct the significant activities of Entity Y but does not absorb any expected losses and has no right to receive expected residual returns. Under ASC 810, is Entity X the primary beneficiary?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because having power alone is sufficient

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because Entity X must also have the obligation to absorb losses or the right to receive benefits that could be significant to the VIE

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, because power to direct activities always requires consolidation

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because Entity X must absorb a majority of expected losses

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-25-38A, the primary beneficiary of a VIE must satisfy BOTH criteria: (1) power to direct the activities of the VIE that most significantly impact its economic performance, AND (2) the obligation to absorb losses or the right to receive benefits from the VIE that could potentially be significant to the VIE. Having power alone (criterion 1) without meeting criterion 2 does not make an entity the primary beneficiary.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-25-38Aにより、VIEの主たる受益者は両方の基準を満たす必要がある：(1)VIEの経済的業績に最も重要な影響を与える活動を指揮する力、および(2)VIEにとって重要となり得る損失負担義務または利益受領権。力のみ（基準1）を持ち、基準2を満たさない場合、主たる受益者にはならない。

questions.subtopic: Consolidated Tax Return vs Separate Return
questions.stem: Parent and its 80%-owned domestic Subsidiary file a consolidated federal income tax return. Which of the following is a requirement for filing a consolidated tax return under IRC regulations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The parent must own at least 51% of the subsidiary's voting stock

choices.label: B
choices.text: The parent must own at least 80% of the subsidiary's voting power and value of stock

choices.label: C
choices.text: The parent must own 100% of the subsidiary

choices.label: D
choices.text: The parent must own more than 50% of the subsidiary's total assets

questions.explanation: Under IRC Section 1504(a), an affiliated group eligible to file a consolidated tax return requires the common parent to own at least 80% of the total voting power and at least 80% of the total value of the stock of each includable corporation. This is distinct from the financial accounting consolidation threshold of a controlling financial interest (typically more than 50% voting interest). The 80% threshold applies to both voting power and value.
questions.explanationJa: IRC第1504条(a)により、連結税務申告が可能な関連グループでは、共通親会社が各対象法人の議決権総数の80%以上かつ株式価値合計の80%以上を保有する必要がある。これは財務会計上の連結基準（通常は議決権の50%超）とは異なる。80%基準は議決権と価値の両方に適用される。

questions.stem: Parent charges its 100%-owned Subsidiary a $500,000 annual management fee. How is this fee treated in the consolidated financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reported as revenue for Parent and expense for Subsidiary

choices.label: B
choices.text: Completely eliminated in consolidation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reported net as a reduction of the Subsidiary's operating expenses

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reclassified as an intercompany dividend

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-1, all intercompany transactions must be eliminated in consolidation. The management fee of $500,000 recorded as revenue by Parent and as an expense by Subsidiary represents an intercompany transaction with no economic impact on the consolidated entity. The elimination entry debits management fee revenue for $500,000 and credits management fee expense for $500,000, resulting in no net effect on consolidated income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-1により、全ての会社間取引は連結上消去する。親会社が収益として、子会社が費用として計上する管理報酬$500,000は、連結企業体に経済的影響のない会社間取引である。消去仕訳：管理報酬収入$500,000を借方、管理報酬費用$500,000を貸方で計上し、連結利益への影響はない。

questions.stem: Parent lent $2,000,000 to its wholly-owned Subsidiary at 5% annual interest. At year-end, $100,000 of interest has accrued. Which of the following correctly describes the elimination entries needed in the consolidated working papers?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Eliminate the intercompany note receivable/payable of $2,000,000 and the accrued interest receivable/payable of $100,000, and eliminate the interest revenue and interest expense of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Eliminate only the intercompany note receivable/payable; the interest is a real expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Eliminate only the interest revenue and expense; the loan balance is a real asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: No elimination is needed because the loan is at a market rate of interest

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-1, all intercompany balances and transactions must be eliminated in consolidation. This includes both (1) the intercompany loan principal (note receivable on Parent's books and note payable on Subsidiary's books) and related accrued interest receivable/payable, and (2) the intercompany interest revenue recognized by Parent and the interest expense recognized by Subsidiary. Both the balance sheet items and income statement items must be eliminated.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-1により、全ての会社間残高・取引は連結上消去する。これには(1)会社間貸付元本（親会社の受取手形と子会社の支払手形）および未収・未払利息と、(2)親会社が認識した受取利息収入と子会社が認識した支払利息費用の両方が含まれる。貸借対照表項目と損益計算書項目の両方を消去する。

questions.stem: Parent leases a building to its 100%-owned Subsidiary under an operating lease. The annual lease payment is $120,000. On the consolidated financial statements, how should this lease be reported?

choices.label: A
