questions.explanation: ASC 810-10-35-4 lists specific reconsideration events, including changes to the entity's governing documents or contractual arrangements that change the characteristics or adequacy of the equity investment at risk, returns of equity to investors, additional equity issuances, and undertaking additional activities. General market movements such as interest rate changes, the passage of time, and industry downturns are not reconsideration events; the VIE status determination is not continuously reassessed for ordinary changes in economic conditions (in contrast to the primary beneficiary determination, which is assessed on an ongoing basis).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-35-4は、VIE該当性の再判定が必要となる事象（reconsideration events）を限定列挙しており、定款・契約の変更によるリスク負担資本の性質や十分性の変化、出資の払戻し、追加出資、追加的事業活動の開始などが含まれる。市場金利の変動、単なる時の経過、業界全体の景気後退は再判定事象に該当しない。VIE該当性の判定は通常の経済環境の変化では見直されない（一方、第一受益者の判定は継続的に見直される点と対比される）。

questions.stem: Pico Corp (an SEC registrant) owns 80% of Sub Inc. The noncontrolling shareholders hold a put option that allows them to require Pico to purchase their shares for cash at fair value, and the redemption is not solely within Pico's control. How should the noncontrolling interest be presented in Pico's consolidated balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In temporary (mezzanine) equity, outside of permanent equity

choices.label: B
choices.text: Within permanent equity, separate from the parent's equity

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a long-term liability measured at the redemption amount

choices.label: D
choices.text: Netted against the parent's retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under SEC guidance codified in ASC 480-10-S99-3A, equity instruments that are redeemable for cash at the option of the holder, or upon events not solely within the issuer's control, must be classified outside of permanent equity in temporary (mezzanine) equity. A redeemable NCI subject to a holder put option meets this criterion. Presentation within permanent equity applies only to ordinary NCI without such redemption features. The instrument is not a liability because redemption is contingent on the holder's option rather than mandatory, and netting against retained earnings has no basis in GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 480-10-S99-3A（SECガイダンス）により、保有者の選択または発行者の支配外の事象により現金償還され得る持分商品は、恒久資本（permanent equity）の外側、すなわち中間区分（temporary/mezzanine equity）に表示しなければならない。NCI保有者がプット・オプションを持ち償還が親会社の支配外である本問のNCIはこれに該当する。恒久資本内での表示は償還条項のない通常のNCIの場合のみ。償還が義務的ではなく保有者の選択に依存するため負債にも該当せず、利益剰余金との相殺にはGAAP上の根拠がない。

questions.subtopic: Contingent Liabilities Assumed in a Business Combination
questions.stem: In a business combination, the acquiree is a defendant in a pending lawsuit. The contingency arises from a contract, and its acquisition-date fair value is determinable. Under ASC 805, how should the acquirer account for this assumed contingency at the acquisition date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize it only if payment is probable and reasonably estimable under ASC 450

choices.label: B
choices.text: Disclose it in the notes but recognize no liability until settlement

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a liability at its acquisition-date fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a liability equal to the maximum possible loss

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-25-19 through 25-20, an acquirer recognizes an assumed contingency at its acquisition-date fair value if that fair value can be determined during the measurement period. The probable-and-reasonably-estimable threshold of ASC 450 applies in acquisition accounting only when the acquisition-date fair value cannot be determined; here it can be. Disclosure-only treatment understates the liabilities assumed, and the maximum possible loss is not a fair value measure because it ignores the probability of different outcomes.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-25-19〜25-20により、取得日の公正価値が測定期間内に算定可能な場合、取得企業は引き受けた偶発債務を取得日公正価値で負債計上する。ASC 450の「発生の可能性が高く合理的に見積可能」という認識基準が適用されるのは、公正価値が算定できない場合に限られるが、本問では算定可能である。注記開示のみでは引受負債が過小表示となり、最大損失額は各結果の発生確率を無視するため公正価値測定とはいえない。

questions.stem: In acquiring Franchisee Corp, Granter Inc reacquired a franchise right it had previously granted to Franchisee. The reacquired right is recognized as an intangible asset at a fair value of $600,000. The remaining contractual term of the franchise agreement is 4 years, and market participants would expect renewals extending the right for 4 additional years. Over what period and at what annual amount should the reacquired right be amortized (straight-line)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 8 years at $75,000 per year

choices.label: B
choices.text: 4 years at $150,000 per year

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is not amortized because it has an indefinite life

choices.label: D
choices.text: 5 years at $120,000 per year

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-30-20 and 805-20-35-2, a reacquired right is measured and amortized over the remaining contractual term of the contract in which the right was granted, without regard to potential renewals. Amortization = $600,000 / 4 years = $150,000 per year. Including the 4 expected renewal years to reach an 8-year life is prohibited specifically for reacquired rights, even though renewals may be considered for other intangibles. The right has a finite contractual term, so indefinite-life treatment is inappropriate, and a 5-year period has no basis in the facts.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-30-20および805-20-35-2により、再取得した権利（reacquired right）は、更新可能性を考慮せず、当該権利を付与した契約の残存契約期間にわたって測定・償却する。年間償却額＝$600,000÷4年＝$150,000。他の無形資産では更新期間を耐用年数に含め得るのに対し、再取得権利については更新4年を加えた8年での償却は明示的に禁止されている。契約期間が有限であるため非償却（耐用年数不確定）の扱いも不適切であり、5年という期間には事実関係上の根拠がない。

questions.subtopic: Acquiree as Lessor - Off-Market Operating Lease
questions.stem: In a business combination, the acquiree is the lessor under an operating lease of a building, and the lease terms are favorable to the acquiree relative to current market terms. Under ASC 805, how should the acquirer reflect the favorable lease terms?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a separate intangible asset for the favorable terms

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a deferred credit amortized over the lease term

choices.label: C
choices.text: Adjust goodwill for the favorable terms

choices.label: D
choices.text: Incorporate the lease terms into the fair value measurement of the underlying building; no separate intangible is recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-25-12 and related guidance, when the acquiree is the lessor in an operating lease, the acquirer measures the underlying leased asset at fair value taking the lease terms into account; favorable or unfavorable terms are embedded in that measurement rather than recognized as a separate intangible asset or liability. A separate favorable-lease intangible is recognized only when the acquiree is the lessee (where it adjusts the right-of-use asset) under legacy guidance contexts, not for lessor operating leases. A deferred credit applies to unfavorable positions in other contexts, and goodwill is a residual that is not adjusted directly for lease terms.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-25-12等により、被取得企業がオペレーティング・リースの貸手（lessor）である場合、取得企業はリース条件を織り込んだ上で原資産（建物）を公正価値で測定する。有利・不利なリース条件はその測定額に内包され、別個の無形資産・負債としては認識しない。別個の有利リース無形資産が問題となるのは被取得企業が借手の場合（使用権資産の調整として反映）であり、貸手のオペレーティング・リースには適用されない。繰延貸方は別の文脈の処理であり、のれんは残余であってリース条件で直接調整されるものではない。

questions.stem: In a business combination, the acquiree has deferred revenue (a contract liability) from customer contracts with a carrying amount of $300,000 measured under ASC 606. The estimated fair value of the obligation, based on the cost to fulfill plus a market margin, is $220,000. Under ASC 805 as amended by ASU 2021-08, at what amount should the acquirer recognize the contract liability at the acquisition date?
questions.explanation: ASU 2021-08 (codified in ASC 805-20-30-28) requires the acquirer to recognize and measure contract assets and contract liabilities from contracts with customers in accordance with ASC 606, rather than at fair value. The acquirer therefore records the contract liability at $300,000, the amount determined under ASC 606. $220,000 reflects the superseded fair value approach, which previously caused acquired deferred revenue to be written down (the so-called haircut). Recognizing nothing ignores the remaining performance obligations, and $260,000 is merely an average with no basis in the guidance.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2021-08（ASC 805-20-30-28）により、顧客との契約から生じる契約資産・契約負債は公正価値ではなくASC 606に従って認識・測定する。したがって契約負債はASC 606に基づく金額$300,000で計上する。$220,000は改正前の公正価値アプローチ（取得した繰延収益が切り下げられる、いわゆるヘアカット）であり現行基準では誤り。未履行の履行義務が残るため認識ゼロは不適切で、$260,000は単なる平均値であり基準上の根拠がない。

questions.subtopic: Acquired NOL Carryforward - Effect on Goodwill
questions.stem: Quill Corp pays $5,000,000 for 100% of Reed Inc. The fair value of Reed's identifiable net assets, before considering income tax attributes, is $4,200,000. Reed has a net operating loss carryforward for which a deferred tax asset of $300,000 is recognized at the acquisition date (no valuation allowance is needed). What amount of goodwill should Quill recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-740, deferred tax assets of the acquiree, including those for NOL carryforwards expected to be realized, are recognized as part of the identifiable net assets acquired. Identifiable net assets = $4,200,000 + $300,000 = $4,500,000. Goodwill = $5,000,000 − $4,500,000 = $500,000. $800,000 ignores the deferred tax asset. $300,000 is the DTA itself, not goodwill, and $1,100,000 incorrectly subtracts the DTA from net assets instead of adding it.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-740により、実現が見込まれる被取得企業の繰越欠損金に係る繰延税金資産は、取得した識別可能純資産の一部として認識する。識別可能純資産＝$4,200,000＋$300,000＝$4,500,000。のれん＝$5,000,000−$4,500,000＝$500,000。$800,000は繰延税金資産を無視した誤り。$300,000はDTAそのものであってのれんではなく、$1,100,000はDTAを加算ではなく減算した誤りである。

questions.stem: Holdco owns 100% of both Alpha Corp and Beta Corp. Holdco directs Alpha to transfer a manufacturing division to Beta. In Holdco's consolidated records, the division's net assets have a carrying amount of $400,000; their fair value is $600,000. At what amount should Beta record the net assets received?
questions.explanation: A transfer of net assets between entities under common control is not a business combination under ASC 805. Per ASC 805-50-30-5, the receiving entity records the net assets at the carrying amounts in the financial statements of the transferring entity (or the common parent), here $400,000. No new basis of accounting is established and no gain is recognized because there has been no change in ultimate ownership. $600,000 incorrectly applies acquisition-method fair value measurement, $200,000 is the unrecognized appreciation, and $500,000 is an unsupported average.
questions.explanationJa: 共通支配下（common control）の企業間における純資産の移転はASC 805上の企業結合に該当しない。ASC 805-50-30-5により、受入企業は移転企業（または共通親会社）の帳簿価額$400,000で純資産を計上する。最終的な所有関係に変化がないため、新たな会計基礎は生じず利得も認識されない。$600,000は取得法の公正価値測定を誤って適用したもの、$200,000は未認識の評価差額、$500,000は根拠のない平均値である。

questions.stem: Pomo Corp owns 80% of Selt Inc and accounts for the investment using the equity method on its separate books. At December 31, Pomo's separate stockholders' equity is $8,000,000 and Selt's total stockholders' equity is $3,000,000. There is no acquisition differential and no intercompany profit. What is total consolidated stockholders' equity?
questions.explanation: Under the equity method, the parent's separate equity already includes its 80% share of the subsidiary's equity, so consolidated controlling interest equals the parent's equity of $8,000,000. Total consolidated equity adds the noncontrolling interest: 20% × $3,000,000 = $600,000. Total = $8,000,000 + $600,000 = $8,600,000. $8,000,000 omits the NCI. $11,000,000 double counts by adding the subsidiary's full equity to the parent's equity-method balance, and $10,400,000 double counts the parent's 80% share.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法を適用している親会社の単体資本$8,000,000には、子会社資本に対する80%持分がすでに含まれているため、連結上の親会社株主持分は$8,000,000となる。連結資本合計はこれにNCI（20%×$3,000,000＝$600,000）を加えた$8,600,000である。$8,000,000はNCIを欠落させた誤り。$11,000,000は子会社資本全額を持分法適用後の親会社資本に加えた二重計上、$10,400,000は親会社の80%持分を二重計上した誤りである。

questions.stem: Pine Corp owns 90% of Spruce Inc. Pine's common stock is $1,000,000 and Spruce's common stock is $400,000. What amount of common stock should be reported in the consolidated balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Consolidated financial statements present the equity of the parent's shareholders plus a single NCI amount. The subsidiary's common stock is eliminated in full against the investment account (and NCI) in the consolidation working papers, so consolidated common stock is the parent's $1,000,000 only. $1,400,000 fails to eliminate the subsidiary's stock. $1,360,000 incorrectly eliminates only the parent's 90% share, and $1,040,000 incorrectly adds the NCI's 10% of the subsidiary's stock as common stock rather than within the separate NCI component.
questions.explanationJa: 連結財務諸表の資本金は親会社株主のものを表示し、子会社の資本金は連結精算表上、投資勘定（およびNCI）と相殺して全額消去される。したがって連結上の資本金は親会社の$1,000,000のみ。$1,400,000は子会社の資本金を消去していない誤り。$1,360,000は親会社持分90%のみを消去した誤り、$1,040,000は子会社資本金の10%（NCI相当）を、独立したNCI区分ではなく資本金に加えた誤りである。

questions.stem: At December 31, Plat Corp reports accounts receivable of $900,000, which includes $150,000 due from its wholly-owned subsidiary, Sill Inc. Sill reports accounts receivable of $400,000, which includes $50,000 due from Plat. What amount of accounts receivable should be reported in the consolidated balance sheet?
questions.explanation: All intercompany receivables and payables are eliminated in consolidation because the consolidated entity cannot owe money to itself. Consolidated accounts receivable = $900,000 + $400,000 − $150,000 − $50,000 = $1,100,000. $1,300,000 fails to eliminate any intercompany balances. $1,150,000 eliminates only the $150,000 due from Sill, and $1,250,000 eliminates only the $50,000 due from Plat.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-1により、グループ内の債権債務は連結上すべて消去する（連結実体が自分自身に対して債権を持つことはできないため）。連結売掛金＝$900,000＋$400,000−$150,000−$50,000＝$1,100,000。$1,300,000は内部債権を一切消去していない誤り。$1,150,000はSillに対する$150,000のみ、$1,250,000はPlatに対する$50,000のみを消去した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: NCI Attribution with Excess Fair Value Amortization
questions.stem: Pell Corp owns 75% of Sond Inc. At acquisition, the fair value of Sond's equipment exceeded its book value by $200,000; the equipment had a remaining useful life of 10 years (straight-line). For the current year, Sond reported separate net income of $400,000. What amount of consolidated net income should be attributed to the noncontrolling interest?
questions.explanation: The NCI shares in the subsidiary's income adjusted for amortization of the acquisition-date fair value differential. Annual excess amortization = $200,000 / 10 = $20,000. Adjusted subsidiary income = $400,000 − $20,000 = $380,000. NCI share = 25% × $380,000 = $95,000. $100,000 ignores the excess amortization. $90,000 incorrectly doubles the amortization to $40,000, and $105,000 incorrectly adds the amortization to income instead of subtracting it.
questions.explanationJa: NCIへの利益配分は、取得日の公正価値差額の償却を反映した調整後の子会社利益に基づいて行う。超過額の年間償却＝$200,000÷10年＝$20,000。調整後利益＝$400,000−$20,000＝$380,000。NCI帰属額＝25%×$380,000＝$95,000。$100,000は超過償却を無視した誤り。$90,000は償却額を$40,000と二倍にした誤り、$105,000は償却額を減算ではなく加算した誤りである。

questions.stem: Parr Corp paid $1,500,000 for 100% of Sutt Inc on January 1, Year 1. On that date, Sutt's stockholders' equity consisted of common stock of $400,000, additional paid-in capital of $200,000, and retained earnings of $500,000. The fair value of Sutt's land exceeded book value by $150,000, and equipment exceeded book value by $50,000. In the acquisition-date consolidation working paper, what amount of goodwill is included in the investment elimination?
questions.explanation: Sutt's book equity = $400,000 + $200,000 + $500,000 = $1,100,000. Fair value adjustments total $200,000 ($150,000 land + $50,000 equipment), so identifiable net assets at fair value = $1,300,000. Goodwill = $1,500,000 − $1,300,000 = $200,000. $400,000 compares the price only to book equity, ignoring the fair value adjustments. $250,000 reflects only the land adjustment, and $150,000 reflects only the land step-up amount itself rather than the residual goodwill.
questions.explanationJa: Suttの簿価資本＝$400,000＋$200,000＋$500,000＝$1,100,000。公正価値調整は合計$200,000（土地$150,000＋設備$50,000）であり、識別可能純資産の公正価値＝$1,300,000。のれん＝$1,500,000−$1,300,000＝$200,000。$400,000は公正価値調整を無視して簿価資本とのみ比較した誤り。$250,000は土地の調整のみ反映した誤り、$150,000は残余としてののれんではなく土地の評価増額そのものである。

questions.subtopic: Parent's Internal Accounting Method and Consolidated Amounts
questions.stem: Park Corp owns 100% of Sory Inc. Park may account for its investment in Sory on its separate books using either the cost method or the equity method. Which statement best describes the effect of this choice on the consolidated financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidated amounts are identical under either method; only the working paper eliminations differ

choices.label: B
choices.text: Consolidated net income is higher when the equity method is used

choices.label: C
choices.text: Consolidated retained earnings are higher when the cost method is used

choices.label: D
choices.text: The equity method is required on the parent's books whenever the parent consolidates

questions.explanation: The parent's internal bookkeeping method for the investment is a matter of convenience: the investment account, intercompany income, and dividends are all eliminated in consolidation, so consolidated net income, retained earnings, and all other consolidated amounts are identical regardless of whether the cost or equity method is used internally. Only the form of the working paper elimination entries differs. Neither income nor retained earnings differs in the final consolidated statements, and GAAP does not mandate a particular internal method for a subsidiary that will be consolidated.
questions.explanationJa: 親会社が単体帳簿で投資勘定に用いる方法（原価法・持分法）は内部的な便宜の問題にすぎない。連結上は投資勘定、グループ内損益、配当がすべて消去されるため、どちらの方法でも連結純利益・連結利益剰余金を含む連結数値は同一になる。異なるのは連結精算表上の消去仕訳の形だけである。連結対象子会社について、GAAPは親会社の単体帳簿上の処理方法を特定の方法に強制していない。

questions.stem: Prim Corp owns 80% of Sage Inc. The carrying amount of Sage's net assets in the consolidated financial statements is $2,500,000, and the carrying amount of the noncontrolling interest is $480,000. Prim sells its entire 80% interest to an unrelated party for $2,400,000 cash and retains no interest. What amount of gain or loss should Prim recognize on deconsolidation?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-40-5, the deconsolidation gain = (fair value of consideration received + fair value of any retained interest + carrying amount of NCI) − carrying amount of the subsidiary's net assets = ($2,400,000 + $0 + $480,000) − $2,500,000 = $380,000. The $400,000 amount incorrectly compares the proceeds with only 80% of the net assets ($2,000,000) without using the prescribed formula. A $100,000 loss incorrectly compares proceeds with 100% of net assets while ignoring the derecognition of the NCI, and $880,000 omits the net assets' carrying amount reduction for the NCI improperly (adding NCI but deducting only $2,000,000).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-40-5により、支配喪失時の損益＝（受取対価の公正価値＋残存持分の公正価値＋NCIの帳簿価額）−子会社純資産の帳簿価額＝($2,400,000＋$0＋$480,000)−$2,500,000＝$380,000の利得。$400,000は所定の算式を使わず売却対価と純資産の80%（$2,000,000）のみを比較した誤り。$100,000の損失はNCIの認識中止を無視して対価と純資産全額を比較した誤り、$880,000はNCIを加算しつつ純資産を$2,000,000しか控除しない誤った組合せである。

questions.subtopic: Statement of Cash Flows - Stock Issued as Consideration
questions.stem: Pyle Corp acquires 100% of Trent Inc by issuing its own common stock directly to Trent's shareholders. No cash is paid. How should this acquisition be reflected in Pyle's consolidated statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a cash outflow from investing activities equal to the fair value of the stock issued

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a noncash investing and financing activity disclosed in a supplemental schedule or notes

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a cash inflow from financing activities and an outflow from investing activities of equal amounts

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is omitted from the statement of cash flows and all related disclosures

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-50-3, transactions that affect recognized assets or liabilities but involve no cash receipts or payments are excluded from the body of the statement of cash flows and disclosed as noncash investing and financing activities. Issuing stock to acquire a business is the classic example. Reporting a cash outflow misstates cash flows because no cash was paid. Grossing up equal inflows and outflows fabricates cash movements that did not occur, and omitting the transaction entirely violates the disclosure requirement. Any cash acquired with Trent would appear as a cash inflow in investing activities, but the stock consideration itself is noncash.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-50-3により、認識された資産・負債に影響するが現金収支を伴わない取引は、キャッシュ・フロー計算書の本体から除外し、非資金投資・財務活動として補足スケジュールまたは注記で開示する。株式発行による企業買収はその典型例である。現金を支払っていない以上、投資活動の現金支出として表示するのは誤り。同額の収入・支出を両建て表示するのは実在しない資金移動の捏造であり、開示自体を省略することも開示要求に違反する。なお取得したTrentの保有現金があれば投資活動の現金収入として表示される。

questions.stem: Sub Inc is 60%-owned by Par Corp. During the year, Sub sold inventory to Par at a profit, and a portion remains in Par's ending inventory. What percentage of the unrealized intercompany profit in ending inventory should be eliminated in consolidation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 60%, corresponding to the parent's ownership percentage

choices.label: B
choices.text: 40%, corresponding to the noncontrolling interest's percentage

choices.label: C
choices.text: None, because the sale was upstream

choices.label: D
choices.text: 100%, regardless of the parent's ownership percentage

questions.explanation: ASC 810-10-45-1 requires the elimination of intercompany profit in its entirety; the existence of a noncontrolling interest does not justify partial elimination. The full unrealized profit is removed from consolidated inventory and income. For an upstream sale, the effect of the elimination is then shared between the controlling and noncontrolling interests in the income attribution (60%/40%), but the amount eliminated is still 100%. Eliminating only 60% or 40% confuses the attribution step with the elimination step, and eliminating nothing is contrary to the basic consolidation principle.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-45-1により、グループ内未実現利益は全額（100%）消去する。NCIの存在は部分消去の根拠にならない。アップストリーム取引の場合、消去の影響は利益帰属の段階で支配株主とNCIに60%/40%で配分されるが、消去額自体はあくまで100%である。60%や40%のみの消去は「消去」と「帰属配分」の手続を混同した誤りであり、消去を行わないことは連結の基本原則に反する。

questions.stem: Two operating companies, Delta Corp and Echo Corp, combine their businesses. A new entity, Newco, is formed solely to issue its equity shares to the shareholders of both Delta and Echo to effect the combination. Newco has no other activities. Under ASC 805, which entity should be identified as the acquirer?

choices.label: A
choices.text: One of the combining entities (Delta or Echo) that existed before the combination

choices.label: B
choices.text: Newco, because it is the legal parent that issued the shares

choices.label: C
choices.text: Neither; the combination is accounted for as a pooling of interests

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both Delta and Echo are treated as co-acquirers

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-55-15, a new entity formed solely to issue equity interests to effect a business combination cannot be the acquirer; one of the combining entities that existed before the combination must be identified as the acquirer using the guidance on relative voting rights, board composition, and other factors. Newco's status as legal parent does not make it the accounting acquirer because it is merely a vehicle. Pooling of interests was eliminated from US GAAP, and ASC 805 requires a single acquirer to be identified for every business combination.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-55-15により、企業結合を実行する目的のみで設立され株式を発行する新設企業（Newco）は取得企業になり得ない。結合前から存在する結合当事企業（DeltaまたはEcho）のいずれかを、議決権比率・取締役会構成等の要因に基づき取得企業として識別しなければならない。Newcoが法律上の親会社であっても、単なる器（vehicle）であるため会計上の取得企業にはならない。持分プーリング法は米国GAAPから廃止されており、ASC 805はすべての企業結合について単一の取得企業の識別を要求する。

questions.subtopic: Adjustments After the Measurement Period Ends
questions.stem: Fifteen months after acquiring Tarn Inc, and after the measurement period has closed, Quest Corp obtains information showing that the acquisition-date fair value assigned to an acquired customer list was overstated due to a computational mistake made at the acquisition date. How should Quest account for this?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Adjust goodwill prospectively in the current period as a measurement period adjustment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the entire effect in current period earnings as a change in estimate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Correct the amounts as an error correction in accordance with ASC 250, restating prior periods as applicable

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment is permitted once the measurement period has ended

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-19, after the measurement period ends, the acquirer revises the accounting for a business combination only to correct an error in accordance with ASC 250. A computational mistake existing at the acquisition date is an error, so prior period amounts are restated as required by ASC 250-10-45-23. Measurement period adjustments to goodwill are available only within the measurement period (maximum one year). Change-in-estimate treatment applies to new information about future conditions, not to mistakes that existed at the acquisition date, and GAAP does not prohibit correcting errors merely because the measurement period has closed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-19により、測定期間終了後の企業結合会計の修正は、ASC 250に従った誤謬の訂正に限られる。取得日時点で存在した計算ミスは誤謬に該当するため、ASC 250-10-45-23に従い過年度の財務諸表を修正再表示する。のれんを調整する測定期間調整が認められるのは測定期間内（最長1年）のみ。会計上の見積りの変更は将来の状況に関する新情報に適用されるものであり、取得日に存在したミスには適用されない。また測定期間が終了したからといって誤謬の訂正が禁止されるわけではない。

questions.subtopic: Acquirer's Restructuring Plans - Not Assumed Liabilities
questions.stem: In connection with its acquisition of Sled Inc, Plow Corp plans to close two of Sled's factories and terminate certain Sled employees after the acquisition. Sled has no obligation for these actions at the acquisition date; the plan is Plow's. How should the expected restructuring costs be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized as a liability assumed in the business combination, increasing goodwill

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized as post-combination expenses when the applicable recognition criteria are met

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deducted from the consideration transferred

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognized as a direct reduction of retained earnings at the acquisition date

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-25-2, the acquirer recognizes only the liabilities the acquiree has at the acquisition date. Costs the acquirer expects to incur to exit acquiree activities or terminate employees under the acquirer's own plan are not liabilities of the acquiree at that date; they are recognized as post-combination expenses when the ASC 420 (or other applicable) criteria are met. Recording them as assumed liabilities would improperly inflate goodwill and shield future earnings from the costs. They are not an adjustment of consideration, and charging retained earnings directly bypasses the income statement without basis.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-25-2により、取得企業が認識するのは取得日時点で被取得企業が負っている負債のみである。取得企業自身の計画に基づく工場閉鎖や従業員解雇の見込費用は、取得日時点の被取得企業の負債ではないため、結合後にASC 420等の認識基準を満たした時点で費用として認識する。引受負債として計上すると、のれんが過大になり将来の費用が損益計算書を迂回してしまう。対価の調整でもなく、利益剰余金への直接賦課にも根拠がない。

questions.stem: In a business combination, Brand Corp acquires a competing trade name that it does not intend to use; it acquired the name to prevent others from using it. Under ASC 350-30, how should Brand account for this defensive intangible asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expense it immediately because it will not be actively used

choices.label: B
choices.text: Subsume it into goodwill because it has no direct cash flows

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize it at fair value with an indefinite life not subject to amortization

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize it at fair value and amortize it over the period during which it contributes to cash flows by being withheld from the market

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-30-35-5A, a defensive intangible asset is recognized at fair value and assigned a useful life that reflects the period over which the asset contributes directly or indirectly to the entity's cash flows, which for a defensive asset is the period during which withholding it from competitors provides benefit. The guidance states it would be rare for such an asset to have an indefinite life. Immediate expensing is prohibited because the asset provides economic benefit through its defensive use, and it is an identifiable (separable or contractual) asset that cannot be subsumed into goodwill.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-30-35-5Aにより、防御的無形資産（defensive intangible asset）は公正価値で認識し、競合他社の使用を妨げることで直接・間接にキャッシュ・フローに貢献する期間を耐用年数として償却する。基準上、防御的資産の耐用年数が不確定（indefinite）となるのは稀であるとされている。防御的使用を通じて経済的便益をもたらすため即時費用化は認められず、識別可能（分離可能または契約上の権利）な資産であるためのれんへの包含も認められない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 810, how should net income be presented in a consolidated income statement when a subsidiary has a noncontrolling interest?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidated net income includes amounts attributable to both the parent and the NCI, with the attribution to each presented separately

choices.label: B
choices.text: The NCI's share of income is presented as an expense in arriving at consolidated net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Consolidated net income includes only the parent's share, and the NCI's share is omitted from the income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: The NCI's share is reported in other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-50-1A and 810-10-45-19 through 45-20, consolidated net income is the total for the consolidated entity, including amounts attributable to both the parent and the noncontrolling interest, and the amounts attributable to each must be presented separately on the face of the income statement. Presenting the NCI share as an expense reflects the superseded pre-FAS 160 practice. Omitting the NCI share misstates consolidated net income, and the NCI's share of net income is not an item of other comprehensive income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-50-1Aおよび810-10-45-19〜45-20により、連結純利益は親会社帰属分とNCI帰属分の両方を含む連結実体全体の利益として表示し、それぞれへの帰属額を損益計算書の本体で区分表示する。NCI帰属分を費用として表示するのはFAS 160以前の廃止された実務である。NCI帰属分の省略は連結純利益の誤表示となり、NCIの利益持分はその他の包括利益の項目でもない。

questions.subtopic: Upstream Land Sale - Realization on Sale to Outsider
questions.stem: In Year 1, Slag Inc (90%-owned by Pact Corp) sold land to Pact for $300,000. Slag's cost of the land was $220,000. In Year 3, Pact sold the land to an unrelated third party for $350,000. What amount of gain on the land should be recognized in the Year 3 consolidated income statement?
questions.explanation: From the consolidated perspective, the land's cost is Slag's original $220,000; the $80,000 intercompany gain was deferred in Years 1 and 2. When the land is sold outside the group in Year 3, the entire gain is realized: $350,000 − $220,000 = $130,000 (the $80,000 previously deferred gain plus Pact's $50,000 gain on its books). $50,000 is only the gain recorded on Pact's separate books, ignoring the realization of the deferred gain. $80,000 is only the previously deferred intercompany gain, and $350,000 is the sales proceeds rather than the gain.
questions.explanationJa: 連結の観点では土地の取得原価はSlagの当初原価$220,000であり、グループ内利得$80,000はYear 1〜2で繰延べられていた。Year 3に土地がグループ外へ売却された時点で利得全体が実現する：$350,000−$220,000＝$130,000（繰延利得$80,000＋Pactの単体帳簿上の利得$50,000）。$50,000はPact単体の利得のみで繰延利得の実現を無視した誤り。$80,000は繰延利得部分のみ、$350,000は利得ではなく売却代金である。なおアップストリーム取引のため、実現した$80,000部分はNCIにも10%配分される。

questions.subtopic: Consolidated Depreciation After Fair Value Step-Up
questions.stem: Pram Corp acquired 100% of Sten Inc on January 1, Year 1. On that date, Sten's equipment had a book value of $500,000 and a fair value of $600,000, with a remaining useful life of 5 years and no salvage value (straight-line). Sten recorded depreciation of $100,000 on the equipment in its separate Year 1 statements. What amount of depreciation expense related to this equipment should appear in the Year 1 consolidated income statement?
questions.explanation: In consolidation, the equipment is measured at its acquisition-date fair value of $600,000, so consolidated depreciation = $600,000 / 5 = $120,000 per year. This equals Sten's recorded $100,000 plus $20,000 of excess fair value amortization ($100,000 step-up / 5 years). $100,000 reflects only the book-value depreciation without the step-up. $20,000 is only the incremental amortization, not the total, and $220,000 incorrectly adds the full subsidiary depreciation to the consolidated total, double counting.
questions.explanationJa: 連結上、設備は取得日公正価値$600,000で測定されるため、連結減価償却費＝$600,000÷5年＝$120,000。これはSten計上額$100,000に評価増額$100,000の償却分$20,000（$100,000÷5年）を加えた金額に等しい。$100,000は評価増を反映しない簿価ベースの償却のみ。$20,000は増分償却のみで総額ではなく、$220,000は子会社計上額を連結額にさらに加算した二重計上の誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Consolidated Gross Profit with Downstream Inventory Profit
questions.stem: For Year 1, Prine Corp reported sales of $3,000,000 and cost of goods sold of $1,800,000. Its wholly-owned subsidiary, Slate Inc, reported sales of $1,000,000 and cost of goods sold of $600,000. During the year, Prine sold goods to Slate for $200,000 that cost Prine $140,000; 50% of these goods remain in Slate's ending inventory. What is consolidated gross profit for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Combined gross profit = ($3,000,000 − $1,800,000) + ($1,000,000 − $600,000) = $1,600,000. The intercompany profit on the goods is $200,000 − $140,000 = $60,000, of which 50% ($30,000) remains unrealized in ending inventory and must be deferred. Consolidated gross profit = $1,600,000 − $30,000 = $1,570,000. The sales/COGS elimination of the $200,000 transfer itself has no net effect on gross profit. $1,600,000 ignores the unrealized profit deferral. $1,540,000 defers the full $60,000 even though half the goods were resold, and $1,400,000 incorrectly reduces gross profit by the entire $200,000 intercompany sales amount.
questions.explanationJa: 単純合算の売上総利益＝($3,000,000−$1,800,000)＋($1,000,000−$600,000)＝$1,600,000。グループ内利益は$200,000−$140,000＝$60,000で、その50%（$30,000）が期末在庫に未実現のまま残るため繰延べる。連結売上総利益＝$1,600,000−$30,000＝$1,570,000。$200,000の内部売上高と売上原価の相殺消去自体は売上総利益に正味の影響を与えない。$1,600,000は未実現利益の繰延を無視した誤り。$1,540,000は半分が再販済みであるのに$60,000全額を繰延べた誤り、$1,400,000は内部売上$200,000全額を売上総利益から控除した誤りである。

questions.stem: Parent Corp sold inventory to its wholly-owned subsidiary at a price below Parent's cost, recording a loss. The inventory remains in the subsidiary's ending inventory, and there is no indication that the inventory's market value has declined below Parent's original cost. How should the intercompany loss be treated in consolidation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The loss is eliminated, restoring the inventory to its original cost to the consolidated entity

choices.label: B
choices.text: The loss is recognized because losses, unlike gains, are never deferred in consolidation

choices.label: C
choices.text: The loss is recognized because conservatism requires immediate loss recognition

choices.label: D
choices.text: Half the loss is eliminated and half is recognized

questions.explanation: Intercompany losses are eliminated in consolidation just as intercompany gains are, because no transaction with an outside party has occurred. The inventory is restored to its cost to the consolidated entity. An exception arises when the transaction price reflects an actual impairment in the value of the asset; in that case the loss may indicate a write-down that should be recognized, but the facts state there is no market decline below original cost. The notion that losses are never deferred is contrary to ASC 810-10-45-1, conservatism does not override the elimination requirement when no impairment exists, and a 50% split has no basis.
questions.explanationJa: グループ内取引の損失も利得と同様に連結上消去する。グループ外部との取引が生じていないためである。棚卸資産は連結実体にとっての原価に戻される。例外は取引価格が資産の実際の減損を反映している場合で、その場合は評価減として損失認識が必要となり得るが、本問では市場価値が当初原価を下回る兆候はないとされている。「損失は繰延べない」という考えはASC 810-10-45-1に反し、減損がない以上保守主義も消去要求に優先せず、50%ずつの折衷処理にも根拠がない。

questions.subtopic: Consolidation of Subsidiary with Dissimilar Operations
questions.stem: Industrial Corp, a manufacturer, owns 100% of Finance Sub, a consumer finance company whose operations and financial statement structure differ significantly from Industrial's. Under ASC 810, how should Industrial report Finance Sub?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Use the equity method because the operations are nonhomogeneous

choices.label: B
choices.text: Present Finance Sub's statements separately and not consolidate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Consolidate Finance Sub, because dissimilar operations do not exempt a controlled subsidiary from consolidation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Report the investment at fair value with changes in earnings

questions.explanation: ASC 810-10-15-10 requires consolidation of all entities in which a parent has a controlling financial interest; there is no exception for subsidiaries with operations dissimilar from the parent's (the former nonhomogeneity exception was eliminated by FAS 94). The equity method and separate presentation are not substitutes for consolidation when control exists, and fair value reporting applies to investments where consolidation and the equity method do not, such as certain investment company situations, not to a controlled operating subsidiary.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-15-10により、支配的財務持分を有するすべての企業は連結しなければならず、親会社と事業内容が異質（nonhomogeneous）な子会社に対する例外は存在しない（旧来の異質事業の例外はFAS 94により廃止された）。支配が存在する場合、持分法や個別表示は連結の代替とならない。公正価値での報告は、投資会社など連結や持分法が適用されない投資に用いられるものであり、支配下の事業子会社には適用されない。

questions.subtopic: NCI Attribution with Both Upstream and Downstream Profit
questions.stem: Plex Corp owns 70% of Strand Inc. For Year 1, Strand reported separate net income of $600,000. At year-end, unrealized intercompany profit in ending inventories consists of $40,000 from downstream sales (Plex to Strand) and $50,000 from upstream sales (Strand to Plex). What amount of consolidated net income should be attributed to the noncontrolling interest for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Only upstream unrealized profit affects the NCI, because that profit is in the subsidiary's reported income. Adjusted subsidiary income = $600,000 − $50,000 = $550,000. NCI share = 30% × $550,000 = $165,000. The $40,000 downstream profit is attributed entirely to the parent as the seller. $180,000 ignores both adjustments. $153,000 incorrectly deducts both the downstream and upstream profits from the subsidiary's income, and $168,000 incorrectly deducts only the downstream profit.
questions.explanationJa: NCIに影響するのはアップストリーム取引の未実現利益のみである（その利益が子会社の報告利益に含まれているため）。調整後子会社利益＝$600,000−$50,000＝$550,000。NCI帰属額＝30%×$550,000＝$165,000。ダウンストリームの$40,000は売手である親会社に全額帰属する。$180,000は両方の調整を無視した誤り。$153,000はダウンストリーム分まで子会社利益から控除した誤り、$168,000はダウンストリーム分のみを控除した誤りである。

questions.stem: Pone Corp has bonds payable outstanding with a face amount of $1,000,000 and a carrying amount of $970,000 (unamortized discount of $30,000). Pone's wholly-owned subsidiary purchases all of the bonds on the open market for $1,010,000. What gain or loss should be recognized in the consolidated income statement in the year of purchase?
questions.explanation: From the consolidated viewpoint, the purchase constructively retires the debt. The loss equals the price paid minus the carrying amount of the bonds: $1,010,000 − $970,000 = $40,000 loss, recognized in consolidated income in the year of purchase. A $10,000 loss incorrectly compares the purchase price with the face amount rather than the carrying amount. A $30,000 loss is merely the unamortized discount. A $40,000 gain reverses the direction: paying more than carrying amount to extinguish debt produces a loss, not a gain.
questions.explanationJa: 連結の観点では、子会社による購入は社債の推定償還（constructive retirement）に該当する。損失＝購入価額−社債の帳簿価額＝$1,010,000−$970,000＝$40,000の損失で、購入年度の連結損益に認識する。$10,000の損失は帳簿価額ではなく額面と比較した誤り。$30,000は未償却割引額そのものにすぎない。帳簿価額を上回る金額で債務を消滅させた場合は損失であり、$40,000の利得は方向を逆にした誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Full Goodwill with NCI Fair Value Given
questions.stem: Pyrite Corp acquires 80% of Slate Co's voting stock for $4,000,000 cash. On the acquisition date, the fair value of the 20% noncontrolling interest is $950,000, and the fair value of Slate's identifiable net assets is $4,500,000. Under ASC 805, what amount of goodwill should be recognized in the consolidated financial statements?
questions.explanation: Under U.S. GAAP, NCI is measured at acquisition-date fair value, so goodwill = consideration transferred + fair value of NCI − fair value of identifiable net assets = $4,000,000 + $950,000 − $4,500,000 = $450,000. The $400,000 figure results from measuring NCI at its proportionate share of identifiable net assets (20% × $4,500,000 = $900,000), which is an IFRS option but not permitted under U.S. GAAP. The $950,000 figure is simply the NCI fair value, not goodwill, and $500,000 has no valid basis under the acquisition method.
questions.explanationJa: 米国GAAPでは非支配持分（NCI）を取得日の公正価値で測定するため、のれん＝移転対価＋NCI公正価値−識別可能純資産の公正価値＝$4,000,000＋$950,000−$4,500,000＝$450,000。$400,000はNCIを識別可能純資産の持分比率相当額（20%×$4,500,000＝$900,000）で測定した場合の金額で、これはIFRSでは選択可能だが米国GAAPでは認められない。$950,000はNCIの公正価値そのものであり、のれんではない。

questions.subtopic: Contingent Consideration - Post-Acquisition Fair Value Change
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Quarry Corp acquired Trell Inc and recognized a liability-classified contingent consideration arrangement at its acquisition-date fair value of $300,000. At December 31, Year 1, the fair value of the contingent consideration is $420,000. The increase results from Trell's actual post-acquisition sales performance exceeding expectations, not from new information about facts existing at the acquisition date. How should Quarry account for the $120,000 increase?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase goodwill by $120,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a $120,000 loss in current earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record a $120,000 reduction of additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer recognition until the contingency is settled in cash

questions.explanation: Liability-classified contingent consideration is remeasured to fair value each reporting period, with changes recognized in earnings. Because the $120,000 increase arises from a post-acquisition event (actual performance), it is a $120,000 loss in current earnings. Adjusting goodwill is appropriate only for measurement-period adjustments that reflect new information about facts existing at the acquisition date. Charging additional paid-in capital applies to no contingent consideration scenario, and waiting until cash settlement would ignore the required periodic fair value remeasurement.
questions.explanationJa: 負債に分類された条件付対価は各報告期間末に公正価値で再測定し、変動額を損益に計上する。本問の$120,000の増加は取得後の事象（実際の業績）によるものなので、当期の損失$120,000として認識する。のれんの修正が認められるのは、取得日時点で存在した事実に関する新情報を反映する測定期間の修正のみ。資本剰余金の調整に該当する場面ではなく、現金決済まで認識を繰り延べる処理は毎期の公正価値再測定の要求に反する。

questions.subtopic: Consideration Transferred - Cash, Shares, and Contingent Consideration
questions.stem: Ridge Corp acquires 100% of Toll Inc. Ridge pays $2,500,000 cash, issues 100,000 shares of its $1 par common stock (market price $30 per share on the acquisition date), and agrees to a contingent payment with an acquisition-date fair value of $400,000. The fair value of Toll's identifiable net assets is $5,000,000. What amount of goodwill should Ridge recognize?
questions.explanation: Consideration transferred = cash $2,500,000 + shares at fair value (100,000 × $30 = $3,000,000) + contingent consideration at acquisition-date fair value $400,000 = $5,900,000. Goodwill = $5,900,000 − $5,000,000 = $900,000. The $500,000 figure omits the contingent consideration, which must be included at fair value on the acquisition date. The $2,500,000 figure incorrectly values the shares at par ($100,000) rather than market value. A zero-goodwill answer would require consideration equal to net asset fair value, which is not the case here.
questions.explanationJa: 移転対価＝現金$2,500,000＋株式の公正価値（100,000株×$30＝$3,000,000）＋条件付対価の取得日公正価値$400,000＝$5,900,000。のれん＝$5,900,000−$5,000,000＝$900,000。$500,000は条件付対価を除外した誤り（条件付対価は取得日に公正価値で対価に含める）。$2,500,000は株式を時価ではなく額面（$100,000）で評価した誤り。

questions.subtopic: Debt Issuance Costs in a Business Combination
questions.stem: To finance a business combination, Vale Corp issues bonds and incurs $60,000 of bond issuance costs. Vale also incurs $100,000 of due diligence fees and $40,000 of costs to register the equity shares issued as part of the consideration. Under ASC 805, how should the $60,000 of bond issuance costs be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expensed as incurred in the period of the acquisition

choices.label: B
choices.text: Included in the consideration transferred, increasing goodwill

choices.label: C
choices.text: Charged against additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recorded as a direct deduction from the carrying amount of the bonds and amortized

questions.explanation: Costs to issue debt in a business combination are accounted for under the debt guidance: they are presented as a direct deduction from the carrying amount of the debt and amortized to interest expense using the effective interest method. Expensing as incurred applies to the $100,000 of due diligence (acquisition-related) costs, and charging against additional paid-in capital applies to the $40,000 of equity registration costs. None of these costs are part of consideration transferred, so they never increase goodwill.
questions.explanationJa: 企業結合に伴う負債（社債）の発行費用は負債の会計基準に従い、社債の帳簿価額からの直接控除として表示し、実効金利法で利息費用に償却する。発生時に費用処理するのはデューデリジェンス費用$100,000などの取得関連コストであり、資本剰余金から控除するのは株式発行登録費用$40,000である。これらのコストはいずれも移転対価には含まれないため、のれんを増加させることはない。

questions.stem: Wren Corp acquires 90% of Sill Inc for $1,700,000. The acquisition-date fair value of the 10% noncontrolling interest is $200,000. After Wren reassesses the identification and measurement of all assets acquired and liabilities assumed, the fair value of Sill's identifiable net assets is $2,150,000. What amount of bargain purchase gain should Wren recognize, and to whom is it attributed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $450,000, attributed proportionately to the acquirer and the NCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: $250,000, attributed entirely to the acquirer

choices.label: C
choices.text: $225,000, attributed entirely to the acquirer

choices.label: D
choices.text: $250,000, attributed proportionately to the acquirer and the NCI

questions.explanation: Bargain purchase gain = fair value of identifiable net assets − (consideration transferred + fair value of NCI) = $2,150,000 − ($1,700,000 + $200,000) = $250,000. ASC 805 attributes the entire bargain purchase gain to the acquirer; none is allocated to the noncontrolling interest. The $450,000 figure ignores the NCI fair value ($2,150,000 − $1,700,000), and the $225,000 figure incorrectly multiplies the gain by the 90% ownership percentage. Any allocation of the gain to the NCI is inconsistent with the standard.
questions.explanationJa: 廉価購入益＝識別可能純資産の公正価値−（移転対価＋NCI公正価値）＝$2,150,000−（$1,700,000＋$200,000）＝$250,000。ASC 805では廉価購入益は全額を取得企業に帰属させ、NCIには一切配分しない。$450,000はNCI公正価値を無視した計算（$2,150,000−$1,700,000）、$225,000は利得に持分比率90%を乗じた誤りである。

questions.stem: Pyx Corp acquires 60% of Sora Inc for $720,000 when the fair value of the 40% noncontrolling interest is $470,000 and the fair value of Sora's identifiable net assets is $1,000,000. Under the acquisition method, what amount of the total goodwill is attributable to the noncontrolling interest?
questions.explanation: Total goodwill = ($720,000 + $470,000) − $1,000,000 = $190,000. Goodwill attributable to the NCI = NCI fair value − NCI's share of identifiable net assets = $470,000 − (40% × $1,000,000) = $70,000. Goodwill attributable to the parent = $720,000 − $600,000 = $120,000. The $76,000 figure incorrectly allocates total goodwill by the 40% ownership ratio; goodwill is not necessarily allocated proportionately because the parent typically pays a control premium. The $190,000 figure is total goodwill, not the NCI portion.
questions.explanationJa: のれん総額＝（$720,000＋$470,000）−$1,000,000＝$190,000。NCIに帰属するのれん＝NCI公正価値−識別可能純資産のNCI持分＝$470,000−（40%×$1,000,000）＝$70,000。親会社帰属分は$720,000−$600,000＝$120,000。$76,000はのれん総額を持分比率40%で按分した誤りで、親会社は通常支配プレミアムを支払うため、のれんは持分比率どおりには配分されない。$190,000はのれん総額でありNCI帰属分ではない。

questions.stem: Pell Corp sells inventory to its wholly-owned subsidiary, Sand Inc, at a markup of 20% on Pell's cost. At December 31, Year 1, Sand's inventory includes goods purchased from Pell at a transfer price of $144,000. What amount of intercompany profit should be eliminated in the Year 1 consolidated financial statements?
questions.explanation: Because the markup is 20% on cost, the transfer price of $144,000 equals 120% of Pell's cost. Cost = $144,000 ÷ 1.20 = $120,000, so the unrealized intercompany profit = $144,000 − $120,000 = $24,000. The $28,800 figure incorrectly applies 20% to the transfer price ($144,000 × 20%) instead of working back to cost. The $120,000 figure is Pell's cost, not the profit, and $12,000 has no basis in the markup structure.
questions.explanationJa: マークアップが原価の20%なので、振替価格$144,000は原価の120%に相当する。原価＝$144,000÷1.20＝$120,000、未実現利益＝$144,000−$120,000＝$24,000。$28,800は振替価格に直接20%を乗じた誤り（$144,000×20%）で、原価に遡って計算していない。$120,000は原価そのものであり利益ではない。

questions.subtopic: Intercompany Equipment Sale - Year 2 Income Effect
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Plover Corp sold equipment to its wholly-owned subsidiary at a gain of $60,000. On the sale date, the equipment had a remaining useful life of 5 years with no salvage value, and the subsidiary depreciates it on a straight-line basis. For Year 2, how will consolidated net income compare with the simple sum of the two companies' separate net incomes?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidated net income will be lower by $48,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Consolidated net income will be lower by $12,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Consolidated net income will be higher by $12,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Consolidated net income will be higher by $48,000

questions.explanation: The $60,000 gain was eliminated in Year 1 and is realized through use at $60,000 ÷ 5 = $12,000 per year. In Year 2, the only consolidation adjustment is the reduction of depreciation expense by $12,000 (the subsidiary's depreciation is based on the inflated transfer price), so consolidated net income is $12,000 higher than the sum of the separate incomes. A $48,000 difference describes the remaining unrealized gain at the end of Year 1, which affects the cumulative balance, not the Year 2 income effect. A downward adjustment of $12,000 reverses the direction: the elimination of excess depreciation increases, not decreases, consolidated income.
questions.explanationJa: 利得$60,000はYear 1に消去され、使用を通じて毎年$60,000÷5＝$12,000ずつ実現する。Year 2の連結修正は減価償却費の$12,000の減額のみ（子会社の償却費は割高な振替価格に基づくため）であり、連結純利益は個別純利益の合計より$12,000多くなる。$48,000はYear 1末時点の未実現利得残高であり、Year 2の損益への影響額ではない。過大な減価償却費の消去は連結利益を増加させるのであって減少させるのではない。

questions.subtopic: Intercompany Bonds - Partial Purchase Constructive Gain
questions.stem: Prism Corp has bonds outstanding with a total face amount of $1,000,000 and a carrying amount of $1,020,000 (unamortized premium of $20,000). Prism's wholly-owned subsidiary purchases 60% of the face amount of these bonds on the open market for $588,000. What constructive gain or loss should be recognized in the consolidated income statement in the year of purchase?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0; no gain or loss is recognized until the bonds are legally retired

questions.explanation: From a consolidated perspective, the purchase constructively retires 60% of the issue. The carrying amount of the retired portion = 60% × $1,020,000 = $612,000. Constructive gain = $612,000 − $588,000 (price paid) = $24,000. The $12,000 figure compares the price only with face value ($600,000 − $588,000), ignoring the premium. The $36,000 figure double-counts by adding the premium portion to that result. Deferring recognition until legal retirement is incorrect: the gain is recognized in the period the affiliate acquires the bonds because the debt is extinguished from the consolidated entity's viewpoint.
questions.explanationJa: 連結上、この購入により社債の60%が実質的に償還（constructive retirement）される。償還部分の帳簿価額＝60%×$1,020,000＝$612,000。実質償還益＝$612,000−$588,000＝$24,000。$12,000は額面のみと比較した誤り（$600,000−$588,000）でプレミアムを無視している。法的償還まで認識を繰り延べる処理は誤りで、連結グループの観点では関係会社が社債を取得した期に債務が消滅したものとして利得を認識する。

questions.subtopic: VIE - Characteristics of a Variable Interest Entity
questions.stem: Under ASC 810-10, which of the following characteristics, by itself, indicates that a legal entity is a variable interest entity (VIE)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The holders of the equity investment at risk, as a group, lack the power through voting or similar rights to direct the activities that most significantly impact the entity's economic performance

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity is financed primarily with equity contributed by its investors

choices.label: C
choices.text: The equity investors at risk absorb the entity's expected losses and receive its expected residual returns

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entity is a corporation with a single shareholder owning a majority of the voting stock

questions.explanation: An entity is a VIE if its equity investment at risk is insufficient to finance its activities without additional subordinated financial support, or if the equity holders at risk, as a group, lack any of the characteristics of a controlling financial interest — including the power through voting rights to direct the entity's most significant activities. When equity holders do absorb expected losses and receive residual returns, that is a characteristic of a typical voting interest entity, not a VIE. Being financed primarily with equity and having a majority shareholder both point toward the voting interest model rather than VIE status.
questions.explanationJa: 事業体がVIEに該当するのは、リスクにさらされている持分投資が追加的な劣後支援なしに活動を賄うのに不十分な場合、または持分投資者がグループとして支配的財務持分の特徴（議決権を通じて最も重要な活動を指図する力など）を欠く場合である。持分投資者が期待損失を負担し残余リターンを受け取ることは、通常の議決権モデルの事業体の特徴でありVIEの兆候ではない。主に持分で資金調達されていることや過半数株主の存在も、VIEではなく議決権モデルを示唆する。

questions.subtopic: Majority Ownership with Substantive Participating Rights of Minority
questions.stem: Pavo Corp owns 60% of the voting stock of Trine Inc. Under a shareholder agreement, the 40% minority shareholder has the substantive right to approve Trine's annual operating budget and to hire and terminate Trine's management responsible for implementing its policies. How should Pavo account for its investment in Trine?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidate Trine, because Pavo holds a majority voting interest

choices.label: B
choices.text: Consolidate Trine, but disclose the minority's rights in the notes

choices.label: C
choices.text: Apply the equity method, because the minority's substantive participating rights overcome the presumption of control

choices.label: D
choices.text: Apply the fair value option, because control is shared

questions.explanation: Majority voting ownership creates a presumption of control, but that presumption is overcome when noncontrolling shareholders hold substantive participating rights — such as approving operating budgets and hiring or firing management responsible for implementing the entity's policies. Because Pavo cannot unilaterally direct Trine's significant activities, it does not consolidate and instead applies the equity method given its significant influence. Consolidating with mere disclosure ignores that the participating rights negate control itself. The fair value option is an election for certain financial instruments, not a required treatment driven by shared control.
questions.explanationJa: 議決権の過半数所有は支配の推定を生むが、少数株主が実質的な参加権（事業予算の承認、方針を実行する経営陣の任免など）を有する場合、その推定は覆される。PavoはTrineの重要な活動を単独で指図できないため連結せず、重要な影響力に基づき持分法を適用する。注記開示をしつつ連結する処理は、参加権が支配そのものを否定する点を見落としている。公正価値オプションは特定の金融商品に対する選択であり、支配の共有によって強制される処理ではない。

questions.stem: Acquirer Corp obtains control of Target Inc in a transaction that results in a bargain purchase. Target elects to apply push-down accounting in its separate financial statements. How should Target reflect the bargain purchase gain in its separate financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a gain in its separate income statement, consistent with the acquirer's treatment

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a direct credit to retained earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a deferred credit amortized over the useful lives of the acquired assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an adjustment to additional paid-in capital, not as income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-50, an acquiree that applies push-down accounting recognizes the acquirer's new basis in its separate statements, but any bargain purchase gain recognized by the acquirer is reflected by the acquiree as an adjustment to additional paid-in capital — it is not recognized in the acquiree's income statement. Recognizing the gain in income is the acquirer's treatment, not the acquiree's. A direct credit to retained earnings and a deferred credit amortized over time are both inconsistent with the codified push-down guidance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-50に基づき、プッシュダウン会計を選択した被取得企業は取得企業の新たな測定基礎を個別財務諸表に反映するが、取得企業が認識した廉価購入益は被取得企業では資本剰余金（APIC）の調整として処理し、損益計算書では認識しない。利得を損益に計上するのは取得企業側の処理である。利益剰余金への直接貸記や、取得資産の耐用年数にわたり償却する繰延貸方処理は、いずれも基準の定めに反する。

questions.subtopic: Step Acquisition - Previously Held Interest Not on Equity Method
questions.stem: Quill Corp holds a 10% investment in Tarn Inc, carried at $150,000 under the measurement alternative for equity securities without readily determinable fair values. On July 1, Year 2, Quill acquires an additional 90% of Tarn for $2,520,000, obtaining control. On that date, the fair value of the previously held 10% interest is $280,000. What amount of gain should Quill recognize on the previously held interest at the acquisition date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0; remeasurement applies only to previously held equity-method investments

questions.explanation: In a business combination achieved in stages, the acquirer remeasures any previously held equity interest to its acquisition-date fair value and recognizes the difference in earnings, regardless of how the interest was previously accounted for. Gain = $280,000 − $150,000 = $130,000. Limiting remeasurement to equity-method investments is a misconception; the requirement covers all previously held interests. The $280,000 figure is the fair value itself rather than the gain, and $13,000 incorrectly applies the 10% ownership percentage to the gain.
questions.explanationJa: 段階取得では、取得企業は従来保有していた持分を取得日の公正価値で再測定し、差額を損益に認識する。従来の会計処理方法（持分法か測定代替か）を問わず適用される。利得＝$280,000−$150,000＝$130,000。再測定は持分法投資のみに適用されるという理解は誤り。$280,000は公正価値そのものであり利得ではなく、$13,000は利得に持分比率10%を乗じた誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Loss of Control with Retained Noncontrolling Investment
questions.stem: Pyre Corp owns 100% of Sable Inc. The carrying amount of Sable's net assets in the consolidated financial statements is $3,000,000. Pyre sells a 60% interest in Sable to an unrelated party for $2,400,000 cash, retaining a 40% interest with a fair value of $1,600,000 on the date control is lost. What gain should Pyre recognize on the transaction?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0; the transaction is an equity transaction

questions.explanation: When control is lost, the parent derecognizes the subsidiary's net assets, recognizes the consideration received and the fair value of any retained noncontrolling investment, and records the difference in earnings. Gain = ($2,400,000 proceeds + $1,600,000 fair value of retained interest) − $3,000,000 carrying amount = $1,000,000. The $600,000 figure considers only the portion sold ($2,400,000 − 60% × $3,000,000) and omits the required remeasurement of the retained interest to fair value. The $400,000 figure isolates only the retained-interest remeasurement ($1,600,000 − 40% × $3,000,000). Equity-transaction treatment applies only to ownership changes that do not result in a loss of control.
questions.explanationJa: 支配を喪失した場合、親会社は子会社の純資産の認識を中止し、受取対価と残存投資の公正価値を認識して差額を損益に計上する。利得＝（売却対価$2,400,000＋残存持分の公正価値$1,600,000）−帳簿価額$3,000,000＝$1,000,000。$600,000は売却部分のみを考慮し（$2,400,000−60%×$3,000,000）、残存持分の公正価値再測定を欠いた誤り。$400,000は残存持分の再測定部分のみ（$1,600,000−40%×$3,000,000）。資本取引としての処理は支配を喪失しない持分変動にのみ適用される。

questions.subtopic: Deconsolidation Without a Sale of Shares
questions.stem: Pine Corp owns 70% of Sumac Inc. Which of the following events would require Pine to deconsolidate Sumac even though Pine has not sold any of its shares?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sumac issues a sufficient number of new shares to outside investors that Pine's interest falls below the level at which it controls Sumac

choices.label: B
choices.text: Pine purchases an additional 10% of Sumac's shares from noncontrolling shareholders

choices.label: C
choices.text: Sumac declares and pays a large cash dividend to all shareholders

choices.label: D
choices.text: Pine pledges its Sumac shares as collateral for a bank loan while retaining voting rights

questions.explanation: Deconsolidation is required whenever the parent ceases to have a controlling financial interest, which can occur without a sale — for example, when the subsidiary issues new shares to third parties and dilutes the parent below control. Buying additional shares from noncontrolling shareholders increases, rather than ends, the controlling interest and is an equity transaction. A dividend reduces the subsidiary's net assets but does not affect control. Pledging shares as collateral while retaining voting rights leaves the controlling financial interest intact.
questions.explanationJa: 親会社が支配的財務持分を失った時点で連結除外（deconsolidation）が必要であり、これは株式売却がなくても起こり得る。たとえば子会社が第三者に新株を発行し、親会社の持分が支配水準を下回るほど希薄化した場合である。非支配株主からの追加取得は支配持分を強めるものであり資本取引として処理する。配当の支払いは子会社の純資産を減らすが支配には影響しない。議決権を保持したまま株式を担保に供することも支配的財務持分を失わせない。

questions.subtopic: Downstream Inventory - Gross Margin on Selling Price
questions.stem: Plume Corp sells inventory to its wholly-owned subsidiary, Swale Inc, at prices that yield Plume a gross margin of 30% of the selling price. At December 31, Year 1, Swale's ending inventory includes $200,000 of goods purchased from Plume. At what amount should this inventory be reported on the consolidated balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Because the gross margin is 30% of the selling price, the unrealized intercompany profit in the inventory is $200,000 × 30% = $60,000. Consolidated inventory is reported at the original cost to the consolidated entity: $200,000 − $60,000 = $140,000. Reporting $200,000 fails to eliminate the intercompany markup. The $60,000 figure is the profit to be eliminated, not the carrying amount. The $153,846 figure ($200,000 ÷ 1.30) incorrectly treats the 30% margin as a markup on cost rather than a margin on selling price.
questions.explanationJa: 売上総利益率が販売価格の30%なので、棚卸資産に含まれる未実現利益＝$200,000×30%＝$60,000。連結貸借対照表上の棚卸資産は連結グループにとっての元の原価で表示する：$200,000−$60,000＝$140,000。$200,000のままでは内部マークアップが消去されていない。$60,000は消去すべき利益であり計上額ではない。$153,846（$200,000÷1.30）は30%を販売価格基準の利益率ではなく原価基準のマークアップと誤解した計算である。

questions.subtopic: Goodwill with Fair Value Adjustments and Unrecorded Intangibles
questions.stem: Quartz Corp pays $5,400,000 for 100% of Trillium Inc. On the acquisition date, the book value of Trillium's net assets is $4,000,000. Trillium's land has a fair value $300,000 greater than its book value, and Trillium has a customer relationship intangible with a fair value of $200,000 that is not recorded on its books. All other assets and liabilities have book values equal to fair values. What amount of goodwill should Quartz recognize?
questions.explanation: Fair value of identifiable net assets = $4,000,000 book value + $300,000 land step-up + $200,000 customer relationship intangible = $4,500,000. Goodwill = $5,400,000 − $4,500,000 = $900,000. The $1,400,000 figure compares the price with book value only, ignoring both fair value adjustments. The $1,100,000 figure includes the land step-up but omits the unrecorded customer relationship, which must be recognized separately because it meets the contractual-legal or separability criterion. The $600,000 figure has no valid derivation.
questions.explanationJa: 識別可能純資産の公正価値＝帳簿価額$4,000,000＋土地の評価増$300,000＋未計上の顧客関係無形資産$200,000＝$4,500,000。のれん＝$5,400,000−$4,500,000＝$900,000。$1,400,000は帳簿価額とだけ比較し公正価値調整を無視した誤り。$1,100,000は土地の評価増は反映したが、契約・法律基準または分離可能性基準を満たすため個別認識が必要な顧客関係無形資産を見落としている。

questions.subtopic: Economic Entity Concept and NCI Classification
questions.stem: Under ASC 810, the noncontrolling interest is reported within consolidated stockholders' equity rather than as a liability or in a mezzanine section (absent redemption features). This presentation reflects which underlying concept of consolidated financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The parent-company concept, under which the statements are prepared solely for the parent's shareholders

choices.label: B
choices.text: The proportionate consolidation concept, under which only the parent's share of each asset is included

choices.label: C
choices.text: The economic entity concept, under which noncontrolling shareholders are owners of the consolidated group

choices.label: D
choices.text: The legal entity concept, under which each company reports only its own equity

questions.explanation: Current GAAP adopts the economic entity concept: the consolidated statements portray the parent and its subsidiaries as a single economic unit, and noncontrolling shareholders are viewed as owners of that unit, so their interest belongs in equity. Under the parent-company concept, the NCI would be presented as a quasi-liability or outside equity, which GAAP rejected. Proportionate consolidation, which would include only the parent's percentage of each asset and liability, is not used for subsidiaries. A strictly legal-entity view would preclude consolidation altogether.
questions.explanationJa: 現行GAAPは経済的単一体概念（economic entity concept）を採用している。連結財務諸表は親会社と子会社を単一の経済的単位として表示し、非支配株主もその単位の所有者とみなされるため、NCIは資本の部に表示される。親会社概念ではNCIを負債類似項目または資本外に表示することになるが、GAAPはこれを採用していない。比例連結（資産・負債を持分比率分だけ取り込む方法）は子会社には用いられない。

questions.subtopic: Mid-Year Acquisition - NCI Share of Income
questions.stem: Prong Corp acquired 80% of Stell Inc on July 1, Year 1. Stell reported net income of $480,000 for the full calendar Year 1, earned evenly throughout the year. There were no fair value adjustments or intercompany transactions. What amount of net income should be attributed to the noncontrolling interest in the Year 1 consolidated income statement?
questions.explanation: Only the subsidiary's post-acquisition results are included in the consolidated income statement. Stell's post-acquisition income = $480,000 × 6/12 = $240,000. NCI share = 20% × $240,000 = $48,000. The $96,000 figure applies 20% to the full-year income, incorrectly including the pre-acquisition period. The $240,000 figure is the entire post-acquisition income before attribution, and $24,000 would correspond to only a quarter-year of NCI income.
questions.explanationJa: 連結損益計算書には子会社の取得後の業績のみを含める。Stellの取得後利益＝$480,000×6/12＝$240,000。NCI帰属分＝20%×$240,000＝$48,000。$96,000は通年利益に20%を乗じた誤りで、取得前期間まで含めてしまっている。$240,000は帰属計算前の取得後利益全体であり、$24,000は3か月分のNCI利益に相当する誤りである。

questions.stem: Finance Co holds a variable interest in specified assets of Vector LLC, a variable interest entity. Those specified assets are the only source of payment for specified liabilities of Vector, and they are essentially walled off from Vector's other assets and creditors. Finance Co has the power to direct the activities of, and absorbs the losses of, only that walled-off portion. Under ASC 810-10, how should Finance Co account for its interest?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidate all of Vector LLC, because a variable interest in any portion requires consolidating the whole entity

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the equity method to its interest in Vector LLC as a whole

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disclose the interest but recognize nothing, because Finance Co holds no equity in Vector

choices.label: D
choices.text: Treat the walled-off portion as a separate VIE (a silo) and consolidate that portion if Finance Co is its primary beneficiary

questions.explanation: ASC 810-10 contains silo provisions: when specified assets of a VIE are essentially the only source of payment for specified liabilities and are walled off from the rest of the entity, that portion is treated as a separate VIE. A reporting entity that is the primary beneficiary of the silo — holding power over its significant activities and the obligation to absorb its losses or right to its returns — consolidates the silo, not the entire host entity. Consolidating all of Vector overstates Finance Co's exposure and power. The equity method and disclosure-only treatments ignore the consolidation requirement that applies at the silo level.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10にはシロ（silo）規定がある。VIEの特定資産が特定負債の実質的に唯一の返済原資であり、事業体の他の部分から隔離されている場合、その部分は別個のVIEとして扱う。シロの主たる受益者（その重要な活動に対するパワーと、損失負担義務またはリターン享受権を持つ企業）は、ホスト事業体全体ではなくシロ部分を連結する。Vector全体の連結はFinance Coのパワーとエクスポージャーを過大に反映する。持分法や開示のみの処理はシロ・レベルで適用される連結要求を無視している。

questions.stem: Riva Corp and Tane Corp each own 50% of the voting stock of Joint Co, and all significant decisions require the unanimous consent of both investors. Neither investor holds any other contractual rights, and Joint Co is not a variable interest entity. How should Riva account for its investment in Joint Co?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidate Joint Co, because 50% ownership is sufficient for control

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the equity method, because neither investor controls Joint Co

choices.label: C
choices.text: Proportionately consolidate 50% of Joint Co's assets and liabilities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Carry the investment at cost with no income recognition until dividends are received

questions.explanation: Under the voting interest model, consolidation requires a controlling financial interest, generally ownership of more than 50% of the voting shares. With a 50/50 split and unanimous-consent requirements, neither investor controls Joint Co, so neither consolidates. Each investor has significant influence and applies the equity method, which is the standard treatment for corporate joint ventures. Proportionate consolidation is not permitted for this structure under U.S. GAAP, and the cost method understates the investor's participation in the venture's results when significant influence exists.
questions.explanationJa: 議決権モデルでは、連結には支配的財務持分（通常は議決権の50%超）が必要。50/50の持分で重要事項に全員一致が必要な場合、いずれの投資者も支配していないため連結しない。各投資者は重要な影響力を有し持分法を適用する。これはコーポレート・ジョイントベンチャーの標準的処理である。米国GAAPではこの形態に比例連結は認められず、重要な影響力がある場合に原価法を用いるとベンチャーの業績への参加が反映されない。

questions.subtopic: Consolidated Retained Earnings - Parent Uses Cost Method
questions.stem: Pavar Corp acquired 80% of Senna Inc on January 1, Year 1, when Senna's retained earnings were $500,000. Pavar accounts for the investment on its separate books using the cost method. At December 31, Year 3, Pavar's separate retained earnings are $2,000,000 and Senna's retained earnings are $900,000. There were no intercompany transactions and no excess fair value amortization. What is consolidated retained earnings at December 31, Year 3?
questions.explanation: Consolidated retained earnings equals the parent's retained earnings as if it had applied the equity method. Because the cost-method parent has not picked up its share of the subsidiary's post-acquisition undistributed earnings, add 80% × ($900,000 − $500,000) = $320,000 to Pavar's $2,000,000, giving $2,320,000. The $2,900,000 figure improperly adds all of Senna's retained earnings to the parent's balance. The $2,400,000 figure adds 100% of the post-acquisition increase, ignoring the 20% that belongs to the noncontrolling interest. The $2,000,000 figure omits the parent's share of the subsidiary's post-acquisition earnings entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 連結利益剰余金は、親会社が持分法を適用していた場合の利益剰余金に等しい。原価法の親会社は子会社の取得後未分配利益の持分を取り込んでいないため、80%×（$900,000−$500,000）＝$320,000をPavarの$2,000,000に加算し、$2,320,000となる。$2,900,000はSennaの利益剰余金全額を単純合算した誤り。$2,400,000は取得後増加額の100%を加算しNCI帰属の20%を無視した誤り。$2,000,000は子会社の取得後利益の親会社持分を全く反映していない。

questions.subtopic: Contingent Payments Tied to Continued Employment
questions.stem: Plato Corp acquires Strive Inc from its founder, who becomes a Strive employee after the acquisition. The purchase agreement provides that Plato will pay the founder an additional $900,000 in three years, but only if the founder remains employed by Strive through the payment date; the payment is forfeited if employment terminates earlier. Under ASC 805, how should Plato account for this arrangement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As compensation expense for post-combination services, recognized over the three-year service period

choices.label: B
choices.text: As contingent consideration measured at acquisition-date fair value and included in goodwill

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an expense of the acquisition recognized in full on the acquisition date

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a reduction of additional paid-in capital when paid

questions.explanation: A contingent payment that is automatically forfeited if the selling shareholder's employment terminates is, by that fact alone, remuneration for post-combination services rather than consideration for the business. It is therefore recognized as compensation expense over the requisite three-year service period. Treating it as contingent consideration would improperly inflate the consideration transferred and goodwill. Expensing the entire amount at the acquisition date ignores the future service condition, and additional paid-in capital is unaffected by a cash compensation arrangement.
questions.explanationJa: 売主株主の雇用終了により自動的に失効する条件付支払は、その事実だけで、事業の対価ではなく取得後の役務に対する報酬と判断される。したがって3年の役務提供期間にわたり報酬費用として認識する。条件付対価として処理すると移転対価とのれんが不当に過大になる。取得日に全額費用処理する方法は将来の勤務条件を無視しており、現金報酬契約は資本剰余金に影響しない。

questions.subtopic: Definition of a Business - Minimum Requirements
questions.stem: Under ASC 805, after an acquired set of assets and activities passes the initial screen test, what must the set include, at a minimum, to be considered a business?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Inputs, processes, and outputs, including an existing customer base

choices.label: B
choices.text: A workforce, real property, and at least one revenue-producing contract

choices.label: C
choices.text: Goodwill or other intangible assets capable of generating returns

choices.label: D
choices.text: An input and a substantive process that together significantly contribute to the ability to create outputs

questions.explanation: To be a business, a set must include, at a minimum, an input and a substantive process applied to that input that together significantly contribute to the ability to create outputs. Outputs themselves are not required — a development-stage set without revenues can still be a business. Requiring inputs, processes, and outputs together (or an existing customer base) overstates the threshold. A workforce or property may be relevant evidence of a substantive process but is not itself a stated minimum, and the presence of goodwill is an indicator considered in the screen analysis, not a definitional requirement.
questions.explanationJa: 事業（business）に該当するには、最低限、インプットと、それに適用される実質的なプロセスが存在し、両者が一体としてアウトプットを生み出す能力に重要な貢献をしている必要がある。アウトプット自体は要求されず、収益のない開発段階の資産群でも事業たり得る。インプット・プロセス・アウトプットの三要素すべて（や既存顧客基盤）を要求するのは基準より厳しすぎる。労働力や不動産は実質的プロセスの証拠にはなり得るが、それ自体が最低要件として定められているわけではない。

questions.subtopic: Intercompany Equipment Sale - Consolidated Carrying Amount
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Prax Corp sold equipment with a carrying amount of $180,000 to its wholly-owned subsidiary for $240,000. On that date, the equipment had a remaining useful life of 6 years with no salvage value, and the subsidiary uses straight-line depreciation. At what net amount should the equipment be reported on the December 31, Year 1 consolidated balance sheet?
questions.explanation: On the consolidated balance sheet, the equipment must appear as if the intercompany sale never occurred: original carrying amount $180,000 less one year of depreciation ($180,000 ÷ 6 = $30,000) = $150,000. The $200,000 figure depreciates the inflated transfer price ($240,000 − $40,000) without eliminating the intercompany gain. The $180,000 figure ignores Year 1 depreciation entirely. The $144,000 figure incorrectly uses a 5-year life on the original carrying amount ($180,000 × 4/5).
questions.explanationJa: 連結貸借対照表では、内部売買がなかったかのように表示する：元の帳簿価額$180,000から1年分の減価償却費（$180,000÷6＝$30,000）を控除し$150,000。$200,000は割高な振替価格を基に償却した金額（$240,000−$40,000）で、内部利得が消去されていない。$180,000はYear 1の減価償却を無視している。$144,000は元の帳簿価額に誤って5年の耐用年数を適用した計算（$180,000×4/5）である。

questions.subtopic: Potential Voting Rights Under the Voting Interest Model
questions.stem: Ander Corp owns 48% of the outstanding voting stock of Brio Inc, which is not a variable interest entity. Ander also holds currently exercisable options that, if exercised, would raise its ownership to 55%. Ander has not exercised the options. Under U.S. GAAP's voting interest model, how should Ander account for its investment in Brio?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidate Brio, because the options give Ander the present ability to obtain majority ownership

choices.label: B
choices.text: Consolidate Brio, because combined actual and potential voting interests exceed 50%

choices.label: C
choices.text: Carry the investment at fair value with changes in earnings, because ownership is below 50%

choices.label: D
choices.text: Apply the equity method, because unexercised options do not provide a controlling financial interest under the voting interest model

questions.explanation: Under the U.S. GAAP voting interest model, control is based on actual present voting interests; unexercised options, warrants, and convertible instruments are not considered in determining whether a controlling financial interest exists. With 48% actual voting ownership, Ander does not consolidate but presumptively has significant influence, so the equity method applies. Consolidating on the basis of potential voting power reflects an approach U.S. GAAP does not use under the voting model. Fair value through earnings is not the default for an investment carrying significant influence unless the fair value option is elected.
questions.explanationJa: 米国GAAPの議決権モデルでは、支配の判定は現実に保有する議決権に基づき、未行使のオプション・ワラント・転換証券は支配的財務持分の判定において考慮しない。実際の議決権が48%のAnderは連結せず、重要な影響力の推定が働くため持分法を適用する。潜在的議決権に基づく連結は米国GAAPの議決権モデルでは採用されていない。公正価値オプションを選択しない限り、重要な影響力のある投資に公正価値（損益経由）は適用されない。
